verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel, If any man's wife goes astray and breaks faith with him,
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man’s wife go aside, and commit a trespass against him,
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that if any man’s wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him

verses.esv: if a man lies with her sexually, and it is hidden from the eyes of her husband, and she is undetected though she has defiled herself, and there is no witness against her, since she was not taken in the act,
verses.kjv: And a man lie with her carnally, and it be hid from the eyes of her husband, and be kept close, and she be defiled, and there be no witness against her, neither she be taken with the manner;
verses.bsb: by sleeping with another man, and it is concealed from her husband and her impurity is undetected (since there is no witness against her and she was not caught in the act),

verses.esv: and if the spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself, or if the spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not defiled herself,
verses.kjv: And the spirit of jealousy come upon him, and he be jealous of his wife, and she be defiled: or if the spirit of jealousy come upon him, and he be jealous of his wife, and she be not defiled:
verses.bsb: and if a feeling of jealousy comes over her husband and he suspects his wife who has defiled herself—or if a feeling of jealousy comes over him and he suspects her even though she has not defiled herself—

verses.esv: then the man shall bring his wife to the priest and bring the offering required of her, a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He shall pour no oil on it and put no frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.
verses.kjv: Then shall the man bring his wife unto the priest, and he shall bring her offering for her, the tenth part of an ephah of barley meal; he shall pour no oil upon it, nor put frankincense thereon; for it is an offering of jealousy, an offering of memorial, bringing iniquity to remembrance.
verses.bsb: then he is to bring his wife to the priest.  He must also bring for her an offering of a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He is not to pour oil over it or put frankincense on it, because it is a grain offering for jealousy, an offering of memorial as a reminder of iniquity.

verses.esv: “And the priest shall bring her near and set her before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall bring her near, and set her before the Lord:
verses.bsb: The priest is to bring the wife forward and have her stand before the LORD.

verses.esv: And the priest shall take holy water in an earthenware vessel and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel; and of the dust that is in the floor of the tabernacle the priest shall take, and put it into the water:
verses.bsb: Then he is to take some holy water in a clay jar and put some of the dust from the tabernacle floor into the water.

verses.esv: And the priest shall set the woman before the LORD and unbind the hair of the woman's head and place in her hands the grain offering of remembrance, which is the grain offering of jealousy. And in his hand the priest shall have the water of bitterness that brings the curse.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall set the woman before the Lord, and uncover the woman’s head, and put the offering of memorial in her hands, which is the jealousy offering: and the priest shall have in his hand the bitter water that causeth the curse:
verses.bsb: After the priest has the woman stand before the LORD, he is to let down her hair and place in her hands the grain offering of memorial, which is the grain offering for jealousy. The priest is to hold the bitter water that brings a curse.

verses.esv: Then the priest shall make her take an oath, saying, ‘If no man has lain with you, and if you have not turned aside to uncleanness while you were under your husband's authority, be free from this water of bitterness that brings the curse.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall charge her by an oath, and say unto the woman, If no man have lain with thee, and if thou hast not gone aside to uncleanness with another instead of thy husband, be thou free from this bitter water that causeth the curse:
verses.bsb: And he is to put the woman under oath and say to her, ‘If no other man has slept with you and you have not gone astray and become defiled while under your husband’s authority, may you be immune to this bitter water that brings a curse.

verses.esv: But if you have gone astray, though you are under your husband's authority, and if you have defiled yourself, and some man other than your husband has lain with you,
verses.kjv: But if thou hast gone aside to another instead of thy husband, and if thou be defiled, and some man have lain with thee beside thine husband:
verses.bsb: But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority and have defiled yourself and lain carnally with a man other than your husband’—

verses.esv: then’ (let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse, and say to the woman) ‘the LORD make you a curse and an oath among your people, when the LORD makes your thigh fall away and your body swell.
verses.kjv: Then the priest shall charge the woman with an oath of cursing, and the priest shall say unto the woman, The Lord make thee a curse and an oath among thy people, when the Lord doth make thy thigh to rot, and thy belly to swell;
verses.bsb: and the priest shall have the woman swear under the oath of the curse—‘then may the LORD make you an attested curse among your people by making your thigh shrivel and your belly swell.

verses.esv: May this water that brings the curse pass into your bowels and make your womb swell and your thigh fall away.’ And the woman shall say, ‘Amen, Amen.’
verses.kjv: And this water that causeth the curse shall go into thy bowels, to make thy belly to swell, and thy thigh to rot: And the woman shall say, Amen, amen.
verses.bsb: May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach and cause your belly to swell and your thigh to shrivel.’  Then the woman is to say, ‘Amen, Amen.’

verses.esv: “Then the priest shall write these curses in a book and wash them off into the water of bitterness.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall write these curses in a book, and he shall blot them out with the bitter water:
verses.bsb: And the priest shall write these curses on a scroll and wash them off into the bitter water.

verses.esv: And he shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse, and the water that brings the curse shall enter into her and cause bitter pain.
verses.kjv: And he shall cause the woman to drink the bitter water that causeth the curse: and the water that causeth the curse shall enter into her, and become bitter.
verses.bsb: He is to have the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse, and it will enter her and cause her bitter suffering.

verses.esv: And the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand and shall wave the grain offering before the LORD and bring it to the altar.
verses.kjv: Then the priest shall take the jealousy offering out of the woman’s hand, and shall wave the offering before the Lord, and offer it upon the altar:
verses.bsb: The priest shall take from her hand the grain offering for jealousy, wave it before the LORD, and bring it to the altar.

verses.esv: And the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering, as its memorial portion, and burn it on the altar, and afterward shall make the woman drink the water.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall take an handful of the offering, even the memorial thereof, and burn it upon the altar, and afterward shall cause the woman to drink the water.
verses.bsb: Then the priest is to take a handful of the grain offering as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar; after that he is to have the woman drink the water.

verses.esv: And when he has made her drink the water, then, if she has defiled herself and has broken faith with her husband, the water that brings the curse shall enter into her and cause bitter pain, and her womb shall swell, and her thigh shall fall away, and the woman shall become a curse among her people.
verses.kjv: And when he hath made her to drink the water, then it shall come to pass, that, if she be defiled, and have done trespass against her husband, that the water that causeth the curse shall enter into her, and become bitter, and her belly shall swell, and her thigh shall rot: and the woman shall be a curse among her people.
verses.bsb: When he has made her drink the water, if she has defiled herself and been unfaithful to her husband, then the water that brings a curse will enter her and cause bitter suffering; her belly will swell, her thigh will shrivel, and she will become accursed among her people.

verses.esv: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, then she shall be free and shall conceive children.
verses.kjv: And if the woman be not defiled, but be clean; then she shall be free, and shall conceive seed.
verses.bsb: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, she will be unaffected and able to conceive children.

verses.esv: “This is the law in cases of jealousy, when a wife, though under her husband's authority, goes astray and defiles herself,
verses.kjv: This is the law of jealousies, when a wife goeth aside to another instead of her husband, and is defiled;
verses.bsb: This is the law of jealousy when a wife goes astray and defiles herself while under her husband’s authority,

verses.esv: or when the spirit of jealousy comes over a man and he is jealous of his wife. Then he shall set the woman before the LORD, and the priest shall carry out for her all this law.
verses.kjv: Or when the spirit of jealousy cometh upon him, and he be jealous over his wife, and shall set the woman before the Lord, and the priest shall execute upon her all this law.
verses.bsb: or when a feeling of jealousy comes over a husband and he suspects his wife. He is to have the woman stand before the LORD, and the priest is to apply to her this entire law.

verses.esv: The man shall be free from iniquity, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.”
verses.kjv: Then shall the man be guiltless from iniquity, and this woman shall bear her iniquity.
verses.bsb: The husband will be free from guilt, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, When either a man or a woman makes a special vow, the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to the LORD,
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When either man or woman shall separate themselves to vow a vow of a Nazarite, to separate themselves unto the Lord:
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that if a man or woman makes a special vow, the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to the LORD,

verses.esv: he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink. He shall drink no vinegar made from wine or strong drink and shall not drink any juice of grapes or eat grapes, fresh or dried.
verses.kjv: He shall separate himself from wine and strong drink, and shall drink no vinegar of wine, or vinegar of strong drink, neither shall he drink any liquor of grapes, nor eat moist grapes, or dried.
verses.bsb: he is to abstain from wine and strong drink. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or strong drink, and he must not drink any grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins.

verses.esv: All the days of his separation he shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine, not even the seeds or the skins.
verses.kjv: All the days of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine tree, from the kernels even to the husk.
verses.bsb: All the days of his separation, he is not to eat anything that comes from the grapevine, not even the seeds or skins.

verses.esv: “All the days of his vow of separation, no razor shall touch his head. Until the time is completed for which he separates himself to the LORD, he shall be holy. He shall let the locks of hair of his head grow long.
verses.kjv: All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no rasor come upon his head: until the days be fulfilled, in the which he separateth himself unto the Lord, he shall be holy, and shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow.
verses.bsb: For the entire period of his vow of separation, no razor shall pass over his head. He must be holy until the time of his separation to the LORD is complete; he must let the hair of his head grow long.

verses.esv: “All the days that he separates himself to the LORD he shall not go near a dead body.
verses.kjv: All the days that he separateth himself unto the Lord he shall come at no dead body.
verses.bsb: Throughout the days of his separation to the LORD, he must not go near a dead body.

verses.esv: Not even for his father or for his mother, for brother or sister, if they die, shall he make himself unclean, because his separation to God is on his head.
verses.kjv: He shall not make himself unclean for his father, or for his mother, for his brother, or for his sister, when they die: because the consecration of his God is upon his head.
verses.bsb: Even if his father or mother or brother or sister should die, he is not to defile himself, because the crown of consecration to his God is upon his head.

verses.esv: All the days of his separation he is holy to the LORD.
verses.kjv: All the days of his separation he is holy unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Throughout the time of his separation, he is holy to the LORD.

verses.esv: “And if any man dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles his consecrated head, then he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing; on the seventh day he shall shave it.
verses.kjv: And if any man die very suddenly by him, and he hath defiled the head of his consecration; then he shall shave his head in the day of his cleansing, on the seventh day shall he shave it.
verses.bsb: If someone suddenly dies in his presence and defiles his consecrated head of hair, he must shave his head on the day of his cleansing—the seventh day.

verses.esv: On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two pigeons to the priest to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
verses.kjv: And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons, to the priest, to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
verses.bsb: On the eighth day he must bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: and the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering, and make atonement for him, because he sinned by reason of the dead body. And he shall consecrate his head that same day
verses.kjv: And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, and make an atonement for him, for that he sinned by the dead, and shall hallow his head that same day.
verses.bsb: And the priest is to offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement for him, because he has sinned by being in the presence of the dead body. On that day he must consecrate his head again.

verses.esv: and separate himself to the LORD for the days of his separation and bring a male lamb a year old for a guilt offering. But the previous period shall be void, because his separation was defiled.
verses.kjv: And he shall consecrate unto the Lord the days of his separation, and shall bring a lamb of the first year for a trespass offering: but the days that were before shall be lost, because his separation was defiled.
verses.bsb: He must rededicate his time of separation to the LORD and bring a year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. But the preceding days shall not be counted, because his separation was defiled.

verses.esv: “And this is the law for the Nazirite, when the time of his separation has been completed: he shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
verses.kjv: ¶ And this is the law of the Nazarite, when the days of his separation are fulfilled: he shall be brought unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
verses.bsb: Now this is the law of the Nazirite when his time of separation is complete: He must be brought to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting,

verses.esv: and he shall bring his gift to the LORD, one male lamb a year old without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb a year old without blemish as a sin offering, and one ram without blemish as a peace offering,
verses.kjv: And he shall offer his offering unto the Lord, one he lamb of the first year without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish for a sin offering, and one ram without blemish for peace offerings,
verses.bsb: and he is to present an offering to the LORD of an unblemished year-old male lamb as a burnt offering, an unblemished year-old female lamb as a sin offering, and an unblemished ram as a peace offering—

verses.esv: and a basket of unleavened bread, loaves of fine flour mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers smeared with oil, and their grain offering and their drink offerings.
verses.kjv: And a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, and wafers of unleavened bread anointed with oil, and their meat offering, and their drink offerings.
verses.bsb: together with their grain offerings and drink offerings—and a basket of unleavened cakes made from fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers coated with oil.

verses.esv: And the priest shall bring them before the LORD and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering,
verses.kjv: And the priest shall bring them before the Lord, and shall offer his sin offering, and his burnt offering:
verses.bsb: The priest is to present all these before the LORD and make the sin offering and the burnt offering.

verses.esv: and he shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of peace offering to the LORD, with the basket of unleavened bread. The priest shall offer also its grain offering and its drink offering.
verses.kjv: And he shall offer the ram for a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord, with the basket of unleavened bread: the priest shall offer also his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: He shall also offer the ram as a peace offering to the LORD, along with the basket of unleavened bread. And the priest is to offer the accompanying grain offering and drink offering.

verses.esv: And the Nazirite shall shave his consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting and shall take the hair from his consecrated head and put it on the fire that is under the sacrifice of the peace offering.
verses.kjv: And the Nazarite shall shave the head of his separation at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall take the hair of the head of his separation, and put it in the fire which is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.
verses.bsb: Then at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, the Nazirite is to shave his consecrated head, take the hair, and put it on the fire under the peace offering.

verses.esv: And the priest shall take the shoulder of the ram, when it is boiled, and one unleavened loaf out of the basket and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them on the hands of the Nazirite, after he has shaved the hair of his consecration,
verses.kjv: And the priest shall take the sodden shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them upon the hands of the Nazarite, after the hair of his separation is shaven:
verses.bsb: And the priest is to take the boiled shoulder from the ram, one unleavened cake from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and put them into the hands of the Nazirite who has just shaved the hair of his consecration.

verses.esv: and the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD. They are a holy portion for the priest, together with the breast that is waved and the thigh that is contributed. And after that the Nazirite may drink wine.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the Lord: this is holy for the priest, with the wave breast and heave shoulder: and after that the Nazarite may drink wine.
verses.bsb: The priest shall then wave them as a wave offering before the LORD. This is a holy portion for the priest, in addition to the breast of the wave offering and the thigh that was presented. After that, the Nazirite may drink wine.

verses.esv: “This is the law of the Nazirite. But if he vows an offering to the LORD above his Nazirite vow, as he can afford, in exact accordance with the vow that he takes, then he shall do in addition to the law of the Nazirite.”
verses.kjv: This is the law of the Nazarite who hath vowed, and of his offering unto the Lord for his separation, beside that that his hand shall get: according to the vow which he vowed, so he must do after the law of his separation.
verses.bsb: This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to the LORD for his separation, in addition to whatever else he can afford; he must fulfill whatever vow he makes, according to the law of his separation.”

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying, Thus you shall bless the people of Israel: you shall say to them,
verses.kjv: Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons, saying, On this wise ye shall bless the children of Israel, saying unto them,
verses.bsb: “Tell Aaron and his sons: This is how you are to bless the Israelites. Say to them:

verses.esv: The LORD bless you and keep you;
verses.kjv: The Lord bless thee, and keep thee:
verses.bsb: ‘May the LORD bless you and keep you;

verses.esv: the LORD make his face to shine upon you and be gracious to you;
verses.kjv: The Lord make his face shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee:
verses.bsb: may the LORD cause His face to shine upon you and be gracious to you;

verses.esv: the LORD lift up his countenance upon you and give you peace.
verses.kjv: The Lord lift up his countenance upon thee, and give thee peace.
verses.bsb: may the LORD lift up His countenance toward you and give you peace.’

verses.esv: “So shall they put my name upon the people of Israel, and I will bless them.”
verses.kjv: And they shall put my name upon the children of Israel; and I will bless them.
verses.bsb: So they shall put My name on the Israelites, and I will bless them.”

verses.esv: On the day when Moses had finished setting up the tabernacle and had anointed and consecrated it with all its furnishings and had anointed and consecrated the altar with all its utensils,
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass on the day that Moses had fully set up the tabernacle, and had anointed it, and sanctified it, and all the instruments thereof, both the altar and all the vessels thereof, and had anointed them, and sanctified them;
verses.bsb: On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it and all its furnishings, along with the altar and all its utensils.

verses.esv: the chiefs of Israel, heads of their fathers' houses, who were the chiefs of the tribes, who were over those who were listed, approached
verses.kjv: That the princes of Israel, heads of the house of their fathers, who were the princes of the tribes, and were over them that were numbered, offered:
verses.bsb: And the leaders of Israel, the heads of their families, presented an offering. These men were the tribal leaders who had supervised the registration.

verses.esv: and brought their offerings before the LORD, six wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for every two of the chiefs, and for each one an ox. They brought them before the tabernacle.
verses.kjv: And they brought their offering before the Lord, six covered wagons, and twelve oxen; a wagon for two of the princes, and for each one an ox: and they brought them before the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: They brought as their offering before the LORD six covered carts and twelve oxen—an ox from each leader and a cart from every two leaders—and presented them before the tabernacle.

verses.esv: Then the LORD said to Moses,
verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Accept these from them, that they may be used in the service of the tent of meeting, and give them to the Levites, to each man according to his service.”
verses.kjv: Take it of them, that they may be to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; and thou shalt give them unto the Levites, to every man according to his service.
verses.bsb: “Accept these gifts from them, that they may be used in the work of the Tent of Meeting. And give them to the Levites, to each man according to his service.”

verses.esv: So Moses took the wagons and the oxen and gave them to the Levites.
verses.kjv: And Moses took the wagons and the oxen, and gave them unto the Levites.
verses.bsb: So Moses took the carts and oxen and gave them to the Levites.

verses.esv: Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershon, according to their service.
verses.kjv: Two wagons and four oxen he gave unto the sons of Gershon, according to their service:
verses.bsb: He gave the Gershonites two carts and four oxen, as their service required,

verses.esv: And four wagons and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their service, under the direction of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
verses.kjv: And four wagons and eight oxen he gave unto the sons of Merari, according unto their service, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
verses.bsb: and he gave the Merarites four carts and eight oxen, as their service required, all under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.

verses.esv: But to the sons of Kohath he gave none, because they were charged with the service of the holy things that had to be carried on the shoulder.
verses.kjv: But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none: because the service of the sanctuary belonging unto them was that they should bear upon their shoulders.
verses.bsb: But he did not give any to the Kohathites, since they were to carry on their shoulders the holy objects for which they were responsible.

verses.esv: And the chiefs offered offerings for the dedication of the altar on the day it was anointed; and the chiefs offered their offering before the altar.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the princes offered for dedicating of the altar in the day that it was anointed, even the princes offered their offering before the altar.
verses.bsb: When the altar was anointed, the leaders approached with their offerings for its dedication and presented them before the altar.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “They shall offer their offerings, one chief each day, for the dedication of the altar.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, They shall offer their offering, each prince on his day, for the dedicating of the altar.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “Each day one leader is to present his offering for the dedication of the altar.”

verses.esv: He who offered his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he that offered his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah:
verses.bsb: On the first day Nahshon son of Amminadab from the tribe of Judah drew near with his offering.

verses.esv: And his offering was one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: And his offering was one silver charger, the weight thereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them were full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.bsb: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

verses.esv: one golden dish of 10 shekels, full of incense;
verses.kjv: One spoon of ten shekels of gold, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.

verses.esv: On the second day Nethanel the son of Zuar, the chief of Issachar, made an offering.
verses.kjv: ¶ On the second day Nethaneel the son of Zuar, prince of Issachar, did offer:
verses.bsb: On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, drew near.

verses.esv: He offered for his offering one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: He offered for his offering one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.bsb: The offering he presented was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

verses.esv: one golden dish of 10 shekels, full of incense;
verses.kjv: One spoon of gold of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nethanel the son of Zuar.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.

verses.esv: On the third day Eliab the son of Helon, the chief of the people of Zebulun:
verses.kjv: ¶ On the third day Eliab the son of Helon, prince of the children of Zebulun, did offer:
verses.bsb: On the third day Eliab son of Helon, the leader of the Zebulunites, drew near.

verses.esv: his offering was one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.bsb: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

verses.esv: one golden dish of 10 shekels, full of incense;
verses.kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab the son of Helon.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Eliab the son of Helon.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.

verses.esv: On the fourth day Elizur the son of Shedeur, the chief of the people of Reuben:
verses.kjv: ¶ On the fourth day Elizur the son of Shedeur, prince of the children of Reuben, did offer:
verses.bsb: On the fourth day Elizur son of Shedeur, the leader of the Reubenites, drew near.

verses.esv: his offering was one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.bsb: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

verses.esv: one golden dish of 10 shekels, full of incense;
verses.kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur the son of Shedeur.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Elizur the son of Shedeur.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.

verses.esv: On the fifth day Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai, the chief of the people of Simeon:
verses.kjv: ¶ On the fifth day Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai, prince of the children of Simeon, did offer:
verses.bsb: On the fifth day Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai, the leader of the Simeonites, drew near.

verses.esv: his offering was one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.bsb: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

verses.esv: one golden dish of 10 shekels, full of incense;
verses.kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

verses.esv: On the sixth day Eliasaph the son of Deuel, the chief of the people of Gad:
verses.kjv: ¶ On the sixth day Eliasaph the son of Deuel, prince of the children of Gad, offered:
verses.bsb: On the sixth day Eliasaph son of Deuel, the leader of the Gadites, drew near.

verses.esv: his offering was one silver plate whose weight was 130 shekels, one silver basin of 70 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
verses.kjv: His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, a silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

verses.kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.bsb: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;

verses.esv: one bull from the herd, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
verses.kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.bsb: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;

verses.esv: one male goat for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.bsb: one male goat for a sin offering;

verses.esv: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
verses.kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
verses.bsb: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.

verses.bsb: On the tenth day Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai, the leader of the Danites, drew near.

verses.bsb: On the twelfth day Ahira son of Enan, the leader of the Naphtalites, drew near.

verses.kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

verses.esv: This was the dedication offering for the altar on the day when it was anointed, from the chiefs of Israel: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, twelve golden dishes,
verses.kjv: This was the dedication of the altar, in the day when it was anointed, by the princes of Israel: twelve chargers of silver, twelve silver bowls, twelve spoons of gold:
verses.bsb: So these were the offerings from the leaders of Israel for the dedication of the altar when it was anointed: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, and twelve gold dishes.

verses.esv: each silver plate weighing 130 shekels and each basin 70, all the silver of the vessels 2,400 shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary,
verses.kjv: Each charger of silver weighing an hundred and thirty shekels, each bowl seventy: all the silver vessels weighed two thousand and four hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:
verses.bsb: Each silver platter weighed a hundred and thirty shekels, and each silver bowl seventy shekels. The total weight of the silver articles was two thousand four hundred shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.

verses.esv: the twelve golden dishes, full of incense, weighing 10 shekels apiece according to the shekel of the sanctuary, all the gold of the dishes being 120 shekels;
verses.kjv: The golden spoons were twelve, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece, after the shekel of the sanctuary: all the gold of the spoons was an hundred and twenty shekels.
verses.bsb: The twelve gold dishes filled with incense weighed ten shekels each, according to the sanctuary shekel. The total weight of the gold dishes was a hundred and twenty shekels.

verses.esv: all the cattle for the burnt offering twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs a year old, with their grain offering; and twelve male goats for a sin offering;
verses.kjv: All the oxen for the burnt offering were twelve bullocks, the rams twelve, the lambs of the first year twelve, with their meat offering: and the kids of the goats for sin offering twelve.
verses.bsb: All the livestock for the burnt offering totaled twelve bulls, twelve rams, and twelve male lambs a year old—together with their grain offerings—and twelve male goats for the sin offering.

verses.esv: and all the cattle for the sacrifice of peace offerings twenty-four bulls, the rams sixty, the male goats sixty, the male lambs a year old sixty. This was the dedication offering for the altar after it was anointed.
verses.kjv: And all the oxen for the sacrifice of the peace offerings were twenty and four bullocks, the rams sixty, the he goats sixty, the lambs of the first year sixty. This was the dedication of the altar, after that it was anointed.
verses.bsb: All the livestock sacrificed for the peace offering totaled twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, and sixty male lambs a year old. This was the dedication offering for the altar after it was anointed.

verses.esv: And when Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the LORD, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim; and it spoke to him.
verses.kjv: And when Moses was gone into the tabernacle of the congregation to speak with him, then he heard the voice of one speaking unto him from off the mercy seat that was upon the ark of testimony, from between the two cherubims: and he spake unto him.
verses.bsb: When Moses entered the Tent of Meeting to speak with the LORD, he heard the voice speaking to him from between the two cherubim above the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony. Thus the LORD spoke to him.

verses.esv: Now the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to Aaron and say to him, When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light in front of the lampstand.”
verses.kjv: Speak unto Aaron, and say unto him, When thou lightest the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light over against the candlestick.
verses.bsb: “Speak to Aaron and tell him: ‘When you set up the seven lamps, they are to light the area in front of the lampstand.’”

verses.esv: And Aaron did so: he set up its lamps in front of the lampstand, as the LORD commanded Moses.
verses.kjv: And Aaron did so; he lighted the lamps thereof over against the candlestick, as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: And Aaron did so; he set up the lamps facing toward the front of the lampstand, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.esv: And this was the workmanship of the lampstand, hammered work of gold. From its base to its flowers, it was hammered work; according to the pattern that the LORD had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand.
verses.kjv: And this work of the candlestick was of beaten gold, unto the shaft thereof, unto the flowers thereof, was beaten work: according unto the pattern which the Lord had shewed Moses, so he made the candlestick.
verses.bsb: This is how the lampstand was constructed: it was made of hammered gold from its base to its blossoms, fashioned according to the pattern the LORD had shown Moses.

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Again the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,

verses.esv: “Take the Levites from among the people of Israel and cleanse them.
verses.kjv: Take the Levites from among the children of Israel, and cleanse them.
verses.bsb: “Take the Levites from among the Israelites and make them ceremonially clean.

verses.esv: Thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: sprinkle the water of purification upon them, and let them go with a razor over all their body, and wash their clothes and cleanse themselves.
verses.kjv: And thus shalt thou do unto them, to cleanse them: Sprinkle water of purifying upon them, and let them shave all their flesh, and let them wash their clothes, and so make themselves clean.
verses.bsb: This is what you must do to cleanse them: Sprinkle them with the water of purification. Have them shave their whole bodies and wash their clothes, and so purify themselves.

verses.esv: Then let them take a bull from the herd and its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you shall take another bull from the herd for a sin offering.
verses.kjv: Then let them take a young bullock with his meat offering, even fine flour mingled with oil, and another young bullock shalt thou take for a sin offering.
verses.bsb: Then have them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you are to take a second young bull for a sin offering.

verses.esv: And you shall bring the Levites before the tent of meeting and assemble the whole congregation of the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring the Levites before the tabernacle of the congregation: and thou shalt gather the whole assembly of the children of Israel together:
verses.bsb: Bring the Levites before the Tent of Meeting and assemble the whole congregation of Israel.

verses.esv: When you bring the Levites before the LORD, the people of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites,
verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring the Levites before the Lord: and the children of Israel shall put their hands upon the Levites:
verses.bsb: You are to present the Levites before the LORD and have the Israelites lay their hands upon them.

verses.esv: and Aaron shall offer the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the people of Israel, that they may do the service of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And Aaron shall offer the Levites before the Lord for an offering of the children of Israel, that they may execute the service of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Aaron is to present the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the sons of Israel, so that they may perform the service of the LORD.

verses.esv: Then the Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and you shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering to the LORD to make atonement for the Levites.
verses.kjv: And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bullocks: and thou shalt offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, unto the Lord, to make an atonement for the Levites.
verses.bsb: And the Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and offer to the LORD one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, to make atonement for the Levites.

verses.esv: And you shall set the Levites before Aaron and his sons, and shall offer them as a wave offering to the LORD.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt set the Levites before Aaron, and before his sons, and offer them for an offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons and then present them before the LORD as a wave offering.

verses.esv: “Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the people of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.
verses.kjv: Thus shalt thou separate the Levites from among the children of Israel: and the Levites shall be mine.
verses.bsb: In this way you shall separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites, and the Levites will belong to Me.

verses.esv: And after that the Levites shall go in to serve at the tent of meeting, when you have cleansed them and offered them as a wave offering.
verses.kjv: And after that shall the Levites go in to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation: and thou shalt cleanse them, and offer them for an offering.
verses.bsb: After you have cleansed them and presented them as a wave offering, they may come to serve at the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: For they are wholly given to me from among the people of Israel. Instead of all who open the womb, the firstborn of all the people of Israel, I have taken them for myself.
verses.kjv: For they are wholly given unto me from among the children of Israel; instead of such as open every womb, even instead of the firstborn of all the children of Israel, have I taken them unto me.
verses.bsb: For the Levites have been wholly given to Me from among the sons of Israel. I have taken them for Myself in place of all who come first from the womb, the firstborn of all the sons of Israel.

verses.esv: For all the firstborn among the people of Israel are mine, both of man and of beast. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I consecrated them for myself,
verses.kjv: For all the firstborn of the children of Israel are mine, both man and beast: on the day that I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt I sanctified them for myself.
verses.bsb: For every firstborn male in Israel is Mine, both man and beast. I set them apart for Myself on the day I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: and I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And I have taken the Levites for all the firstborn of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: But I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel.

verses.esv: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the people of Israel, to do the service for the people of Israel at the tent of meeting and to make atonement for the people of Israel, that there may be no plague among the people of Israel when the people of Israel come near the sanctuary.”
verses.kjv: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the children of Israel, to do the service of the children of Israel in the tabernacle of the congregation, and to make an atonement for the children of Israel: that there be no plague among the children of Israel, when the children of Israel come nigh unto the sanctuary.
verses.bsb: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites, to perform the service for the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting and to make atonement on their behalf, so that no plague will come against the Israelites when they approach the sanctuary.”

verses.esv: Thus did Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the people of Israel to the Levites. According to all that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Levites, the people of Israel did to them.
verses.kjv: And Moses, and Aaron, and all the congregation of the children of Israel, did to the Levites according unto all that the Lord commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so did the children of Israel unto them.
verses.bsb: So Moses, Aaron, and the whole congregation of Israel did with the Levites everything that the LORD had commanded Moses they should do.

verses.esv: And the Levites purified themselves from sin and washed their clothes, and Aaron offered them as a wave offering before the LORD, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
verses.kjv: And the Levites were purified, and they washed their clothes; and Aaron offered them as an offering before the Lord; and Aaron made an atonement for them to cleanse them.
verses.bsb: The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes, and Aaron presented them as a wave offering before the LORD. Aaron also made atonement for them to cleanse them.

verses.esv: And after that the Levites went in to do their service in the tent of meeting before Aaron and his sons; as the LORD had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them.
verses.kjv: And after that went the Levites in to do their service in the tabernacle of the congregation before Aaron, and before his sons: as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so did they unto them.
verses.bsb: After that, the Levites came to perform their service at the Tent of Meeting in the presence of Aaron and his sons. Thus they did with the Levites just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “This applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall come to do duty in the service of the tent of meeting.
verses.kjv: This is it that belongeth unto the Levites: from twenty and five years old and upward they shall go in to wait upon the service of the tabernacle of the congregation:
verses.bsb: “This applies to the Levites: Men twenty-five years of age or older shall enter to perform the service in the work at the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: And from the age of fifty years they shall withdraw from the duty of the service and serve no more.
verses.kjv: And from the age of fifty years they shall cease waiting upon the service thereof, and shall serve no more:
verses.bsb: But at the age of fifty, they must retire from performing the work and no longer serve.

verses.esv: They minister to their brothers in the tent of meeting by keeping guard, but they shall do no service. Thus shall you do to the Levites in assigning their duties.”
verses.kjv: But shall minister with their brethren in the tabernacle of the congregation, to keep the charge, and shall do no service. Thus shalt thou do unto the Levites touching their charge.
verses.bsb: After that, they may assist their brothers in fulfilling their duties at the Tent of Meeting, but they themselves are not to do the work. This is how you are to assign responsibilities to the Levites.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they were come out of the land of Egypt, saying,
verses.bsb: In the first month of the second year after Israel had come out of the land of Egypt, the LORD spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai:

verses.esv: “Let the people of Israel keep the Passover at its appointed time.
verses.kjv: Let the children of Israel also keep the passover at his appointed season.
verses.bsb: “The Israelites are to observe the Passover at its appointed time.

verses.esv: On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall keep it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and all its rules you shall keep it.”
verses.kjv: In the fourteenth day of this month, at even, ye shall keep it in his appointed season: according to all the rites of it, and according to all the ceremonies thereof, shall ye keep it.
verses.bsb: You are to observe it at the appointed time, at twilight on the fourteenth day of this month, in accordance with its statutes and ordinances.”

verses.esv: So Moses told the people of Israel that they should keep the Passover.
verses.kjv: And Moses spake unto the children of Israel, that they should keep the passover.
verses.bsb: So Moses told the Israelites to observe the Passover,

verses.esv: And they kept the Passover in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, in the wilderness of Sinai; according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so the people of Israel did.
verses.kjv: And they kept the passover on the fourteenth day of the first month at even in the wilderness of Sinai: according to all that the Lord commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: and they did so in the Wilderness of Sinai, at twilight on the fourteenth day of the first month. The Israelites did everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.esv: And there were certain men who were unclean through touching a dead body, so that they could not keep the Passover on that day, and they came before Moses and Aaron on that day.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there were certain men, who were defiled by the dead body of a man, that they could not keep the passover on that day: and they came before Moses and before Aaron on that day:
verses.bsb: But there were some men who were unclean due to a dead body, so they could not observe the Passover on that day. And they came before Moses and Aaron that same day

verses.esv: And those men said to him, “We are unclean through touching a dead body. Why are we kept from bringing the LORD's offering at its appointed time among the people of Israel?”
verses.kjv: And those men said unto him, We are defiled by the dead body of a man: wherefore are we kept back, that we may not offer an offering of the Lord in his appointed season among the children of Israel?
verses.bsb: and said to Moses, “We are unclean because of a dead body, but why should we be excluded from presenting the LORD’s offering with the other Israelites at the appointed time?”

verses.esv: And Moses said to them, “Wait, that I may hear what the LORD will command concerning you.”
verses.kjv: And Moses said unto them, Stand still, and I will hear what the Lord will command concerning you.
verses.bsb: “Wait here until I find out what the LORD commands concerning you,” Moses replied.

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel, saying, If any one of you or of your descendants is unclean through touching a dead body, or is on a long journey, he shall still keep the Passover to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If any man of you or of your posterity shall be unclean by reason of a dead body, or be in a journey afar off, yet he shall keep the passover unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Tell the Israelites: ‘When any one of you or your descendants is unclean because of a dead body, or is away on a journey, he may still observe the Passover to the LORD.

verses.esv: In the second month on the fourteenth day at twilight they shall keep it. They shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
verses.kjv: The fourteenth day of the second month at even they shall keep it, and eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
verses.bsb: Such people are to observe it at twilight on the fourteenth day of the second month. They are to eat the lamb, together with unleavened bread and bitter herbs;

verses.esv: They shall leave none of it until the morning, nor break any of its bones; according to all the statute for the Passover they shall keep it.
verses.kjv: They shall leave none of it unto the morning, nor break any bone of it: according to all the ordinances of the passover they shall keep it.
verses.bsb: they may not leave any of it until morning or break any of its bones. They must observe the Passover according to all its statutes.

verses.esv: But if anyone who is clean and is not on a journey fails to keep the Passover, that person shall be cut off from his people because he did not bring the LORD's offering at its appointed time; that man shall bear his sin.
verses.kjv: But the man that is clean, and is not in a journey, and forbeareth to keep the passover, even the same soul shall be cut off from among his people: because he brought not the offering of the Lord in his appointed season, that man shall bear his sin.
verses.bsb: But if a man who is ceremonially clean and is not on a journey still fails to observe the Passover, he must be cut off from his people, because he did not present the LORD’s offering at its appointed time. That man will bear the consequences of his sin.

verses.esv: And if a stranger sojourns among you and would keep the Passover to the LORD, according to the statute of the Passover and according to its rule, so shall he do. You shall have one statute, both for the sojourner and for the native.”
verses.kjv: And if a stranger shall sojourn among you, and will keep the passover unto the Lord; according to the ordinance of the passover, and according to the manner thereof, so shall he do: ye shall have one ordinance, both for the stranger, and for him that was born in the land.
verses.bsb: If a foreigner dwelling among you wants to observe the Passover to the LORD, he is to do so according to the Passover statute and its ordinances. You are to apply the same statute to both the foreigner and the native of the land.’”

verses.esv: On the day that the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the testimony. And at evening it was over the tabernacle like the appearance of fire until morning.
verses.kjv: ¶ And on the day that the tabernacle was reared up the cloud covered the tabernacle, namely, the tent of the testimony: and at even there was upon the tabernacle as it were the appearance of fire, until the morning.
verses.bsb: On the day that the tabernacle, the Tent of the Testimony, was set up, the cloud covered it and appeared like fire above the tabernacle from evening until morning.

verses.esv: So it was always: the cloud covered it by day and the appearance of fire by night.
verses.kjv: So it was alway: the cloud covered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
verses.bsb: It remained that way continually; the cloud would cover the tabernacle by day, and at night it would appear like fire.

verses.esv: And whenever the cloud lifted from over the tent, after that the people of Israel set out, and in the place where the cloud settled down, there the people of Israel camped.
verses.kjv: And when the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle, then after that the children of Israel journeyed: and in the place where the cloud abode, there the children of Israel pitched their tents.
verses.bsb: Whenever the cloud was lifted from above the Tent, the Israelites would set out, and wherever the cloud settled, there the Israelites would camp.

verses.esv: At the command of the LORD the people of Israel set out, and at the command of the LORD they camped. As long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle, they remained in camp.
verses.kjv: At the commandment of the Lord the children of Israel journeyed, and at the commandment of the Lord they pitched: as long as the cloud abode upon the tabernacle they rested in their tents.
verses.bsb: At the LORD’s command the Israelites set out, and at the LORD’s command they camped. As long as the cloud remained over the tabernacle, they remained encamped.

verses.esv: Even when the cloud continued over the tabernacle many days, the people of Israel kept the charge of the LORD and did not set out.
verses.kjv: And when the cloud tarried long upon the tabernacle many days, then the children of Israel kept the charge of the Lord, and journeyed not.
verses.bsb: Even when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle for many days, the Israelites kept the LORD’s charge and did not set out.

verses.esv: Sometimes the cloud was a few days over the tabernacle, and according to the command of the LORD they remained in camp; then according to the command of the LORD they set out.
verses.kjv: And so it was, when the cloud was a few days upon the tabernacle; according to the commandment of the Lord they abode in their tents, and according to the commandment of the Lord they journeyed.
verses.bsb: Sometimes the cloud remained over the tabernacle for only a few days, and they would camp at the LORD’s command and set out at the LORD’s command.

verses.esv: And sometimes the cloud remained from evening until morning. And when the cloud lifted in the morning, they set out, or if it continued for a day and a night, when the cloud lifted they set out.
verses.kjv: And so it was, when the cloud abode from even unto the morning, and that the cloud was taken up in the morning, then they journeyed: whether it was by day or by night that the cloud was taken up, they journeyed.
verses.bsb: Sometimes the cloud remained only from evening until morning, and when it lifted in the morning, they would set out. Whether it was by day or by night, when the cloud was taken up, they would set out.

verses.esv: Whether it was two days, or a month, or a longer time, that the cloud continued over the tabernacle, abiding there, the people of Israel remained in camp and did not set out, but when it lifted they set out.
verses.kjv: Or whether it were two days, or a month, or a year, that the cloud tarried upon the tabernacle, remaining thereon, the children of Israel abode in their tents, and journeyed not: but when it was taken up, they journeyed.
verses.bsb: Whether the cloud lingered for two days, a month, or longer, the Israelites camped and did not set out as long as the cloud remained over the tabernacle; but when it was lifted, they would set out.

verses.esv: At the command of the LORD they camped, and at the command of the LORD they set out. They kept the charge of the LORD, at the command of the LORD by Moses.
verses.kjv: At the commandment of the Lord they rested in the tents, and at the commandment of the Lord they journeyed: they kept the charge of the Lord, at the commandment of the Lord by the hand of Moses.
verses.bsb: They camped at the LORD’s command, and they set out at the LORD’s command; they carried out the LORD’s charge according to His command through Moses.

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Make two silver trumpets. Of hammered work you shall make them, and you shall use them for summoning the congregation and for breaking camp.
verses.kjv: Make thee two trumpets of silver; of a whole piece shalt thou make them: that thou mayest use them for the calling of the assembly, and for the journeying of the camps.
verses.bsb: “Make two trumpets of hammered silver to be used for calling the congregation and for having the camps set out.

verses.esv: And when both are blown, all the congregation shall gather themselves to you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
verses.kjv: And when they shall blow with them, all the assembly shall assemble themselves to thee at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
verses.bsb: When both are sounded, the whole congregation is to assemble before you at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: But if they blow only one, then the chiefs, the heads of the tribes of Israel, shall gather themselves to you.
verses.kjv: And if they blow but with one trumpet, then the princes, which are heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather themselves unto thee.
verses.bsb: But if only one is sounded, then the leaders, the heads of the clans of Israel, are to gather before you.

verses.esv: When you blow an alarm, the camps that are on the east side shall set out.
verses.kjv: When ye blow an alarm, then the camps that lie on the east parts shall go forward.
verses.bsb: When you sound short blasts, the camps that lie on the east side are to set out.

verses.esv: And when you blow an alarm the second time, the camps that are on the south side shall set out. An alarm is to be blown whenever they are to set out.
verses.kjv: When ye blow an alarm the second time, then the camps that lie on the south side shall take their journey: they shall blow an alarm for their journeys.
verses.bsb: When you sound the short blasts a second time, the camps that lie on the south side are to set out. The blasts are to signal them to set out.

verses.esv: But when the assembly is to be gathered together, you shall blow a long blast, but you shall not sound an alarm.
verses.kjv: But when the congregation is to be gathered together, ye shall blow, but ye shall not sound an alarm.
verses.bsb: To convene the assembly, you are to sound long blasts, not short ones.

verses.esv: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets. The trumpets shall be to you for a perpetual statute throughout your generations.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow with the trumpets; and they shall be to you for an ordinance for ever throughout your generations.
verses.bsb: The sons of Aaron, the priests, are to sound the trumpets. This shall be a permanent statute for you and the generations to come.

verses.esv: And when you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, then you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, that you may be remembered before the LORD your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.
verses.kjv: And if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarm with the trumpets; and ye shall be remembered before the Lord your God, and ye shall be saved from your enemies.
verses.bsb: When you enter into battle in your land against an adversary who attacks you, sound short blasts on the trumpets, and you will be remembered before the LORD your God and saved from your enemies.

verses.esv: On the day of your gladness also, and at your appointed feasts and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings. They shall be a reminder of you before your God: I am the LORD your God.”
verses.kjv: Also in the day of your gladness, and in your solemn days, and in the beginnings of your months, ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; that they may be to you for a memorial before your God: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: And on your joyous occasions, your appointed feasts, and the beginning of each month, you are to blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and fellowship offerings to serve as a reminder for you before your God. I am the LORD your God.”

verses.esv: In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the tabernacle of the testimony,
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the twentieth day of the second month, in the second year, that the cloud was taken up from off the tabernacle of the testimony.
verses.bsb: On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year, the cloud was lifted from above the tabernacle of the Testimony,

verses.esv: and the people of Israel set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai. And the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
verses.kjv: And the children of Israel took their journeys out of the wilderness of Sinai; and the cloud rested in the wilderness of Paran.
verses.bsb: and the Israelites set out from the Wilderness of Sinai, traveling from place to place until the cloud settled in the Wilderness of Paran.

verses.esv: They set out for the first time at the command of the LORD by Moses.
verses.kjv: And they first took their journey according to the commandment of the Lord by the hand of Moses.
verses.bsb: They set out this first time according to the LORD’s command through Moses.

verses.esv: The standard of the camp of the people of Judah set out first by their companies, and over their company was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
verses.kjv: ¶ In the first place went the standard of the camp of the children of Judah according to their armies: and over his host was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
verses.bsb: First, the divisions of the camp of Judah set out under their standard, with Nahshon son of Amminadab in command.

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Issachar was Nethanel the son of Zuar.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Issachar was Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
verses.bsb: Nethanel son of Zuar was over the division of the tribe of Issachar,

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
verses.bsb: and Eliab son of Helon was over the division of the tribe of Zebulun.

verses.esv: And when the tabernacle was taken down, the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
verses.kjv: And the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward, bearing the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and the Merarites set out, transporting it.

verses.esv: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out by their companies, and over their company was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the standard of the camp of Reuben set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
verses.bsb: Then the divisions of the camp of Reuben set out under their standard, with Elizur son of Shedeur in command.

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
verses.bsb: Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai was over the division of the tribe of Simeon,

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
verses.bsb: and Eliasaph son of Deuel was over the division of the tribe of Gad.

verses.esv: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things, and the tabernacle was set up before their arrival.
verses.kjv: And the Kohathites set forward, bearing the sanctuary: and the other did set up the tabernacle against they came.
verses.bsb: Then the Kohathites set out, transporting the holy objects; the tabernacle was to be set up before their arrival.

verses.esv: And the standard of the camp of the people of Ephraim set out by their companies, and over their company was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the standard of the camp of the children of Ephraim set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
verses.bsb: Next, the divisions of the camp of Ephraim set out under their standard, with Elishama son of Ammihud in command.

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
verses.bsb: Gamaliel son of Pedahzur was over the division of the tribe of Manasseh,

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
verses.bsb: and Abidan son of Gideoni was over the division of the tribe of Benjamin.

verses.esv: Then the standard of the camp of the people of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out by their companies, and over their company was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the standard of the camp of the children of Dan set forward, which was the rereward of all the camps throughout their hosts: and over his host was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
verses.bsb: Finally, the divisions of the camp of Dan set out under their standard, serving as the rear guard for all units, with Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai in command.

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ochran.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ocran.
verses.bsb: Pagiel son of Ocran was over the division of the tribe of Asher,

verses.esv: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
verses.kjv: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
verses.bsb: and Ahira son of Enan was over the division of the tribe of Naphtali.

verses.esv: This was the order of march of the people of Israel by their companies, when they set out.
verses.kjv: Thus were the journeyings of the children of Israel according to their armies, when they set forward.
verses.bsb: This was the order of march for the Israelite divisions as they set out.

verses.esv: And Moses said to Hobab the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place of which the LORD said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will do good to you, for the LORD has promised good to Israel.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto Hobab, the son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses’ father in law, We are journeying unto the place of which the Lord said, I will give it you: come thou with us, and we will do thee good: for the Lord hath spoken good concerning Israel.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to Hobab, the son of Moses’ father-in-law Reuel the Midianite, “We are setting out for the place of which the LORD said: ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will treat you well, for the LORD has promised good things to Israel.”

verses.esv: But he said to him, “I will not go. I will depart to my own land and to my kindred.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, I will not go; but I will depart to mine own land, and to my kindred.
verses.bsb: “I will not go,” Hobab replied. “Instead, I am going back to my own land and my own people.”

verses.esv: And he said, “Please do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
verses.kjv: And he said, Leave us not, I pray thee; forasmuch as thou knowest how we are to encamp in the wilderness, and thou mayest be to us instead of eyes.
verses.bsb: “Please do not leave us,” Moses said, “since you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you can serve as our eyes.

verses.esv: And if you do go with us, whatever good the LORD will do to us, the same will we do to you.”
verses.kjv: And it shall be, if thou go with us, yea, it shall be, that what goodness the Lord shall do unto us, the same will we do unto thee.
