verses.bsb: If you come with us, we will share with you whatever good things the LORD gives us.”

verses.esv: So they set out from the mount of the LORD three days' journey. And the ark of the covenant of the LORD went before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they departed from the mount of the Lord three days’ journey: and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them in the three days’ journey, to search out a resting place for them.
verses.bsb: So they set out on a three-day journey from the mountain of the LORD, with the ark of the covenant of the LORD traveling ahead of them for those three days to seek a resting place for them.

verses.esv: And the cloud of the LORD was over them by day, whenever they set out from the camp.
verses.kjv: And the cloud of the Lord was upon them by day, when they went out of the camp.
verses.bsb: And the cloud of the LORD was over them by day when they set out from the camp.

verses.esv: And whenever the ark set out, Moses said, “Arise, O LORD, and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee before you.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when the ark set forward, that Moses said, Rise up, Lord, and let thine enemies be scattered; and let them that hate thee flee before thee.
verses.bsb: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say,  “Rise up, O LORD! May Your enemies be scattered; may those who hate You flee before You.”

verses.esv: And when it rested, he said, “Return, O LORD, to the ten thousand thousands of Israel.”
verses.kjv: And when it rested, he said, Return, O Lord, unto the many thousands of Israel.
verses.bsb: And when it came to rest, he would say:  “Return, O LORD, to the countless thousands of Israel.”

verses.esv: And the people complained in the hearing of the LORD about their misfortunes, and when the LORD heard it, his anger was kindled, and the fire of the LORD burned among them and consumed some outlying parts of the camp.
verses.kjv: AND when the people complained, it displeased the Lord: and the Lord heard it; and his anger was kindled; and the fire of the Lord burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the uttermost parts of the camp.
verses.bsb: Soon the people began to complain about their hardship in the hearing of the LORD, and when He heard them, His anger was kindled, and fire from the LORD blazed among them and consumed the outskirts of the camp.

verses.esv: Then the people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to the LORD, and the fire died down.
verses.kjv: And the people cried unto Moses; and when Moses prayed unto the Lord, the fire was quenched.
verses.bsb: And the people cried out to Moses, and he prayed to the LORD, and the fire died down.

verses.esv: So the name of that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the LORD burned among them.
verses.kjv: And he called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the Lord burnt among them.
verses.bsb: So that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the LORD had burned among them.

verses.esv: Now the rabble that was among them had a strong craving. And the people of Israel also wept again and said, “Oh that we had meat to eat!
verses.kjv: ¶ And the mixt multitude that was among them fell a lusting: and the children of Israel also wept again, and said, Who shall give us flesh to eat?
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the rabble among them had a strong craving for other food, and again the Israelites wept and said, “Who will feed us meat?

verses.esv: We remember the fish we ate in Egypt that cost nothing, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic.
verses.kjv: We remember the fish, which we did eat in Egypt freely; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick:
verses.bsb: We remember the fish we ate freely in Egypt, along with the cucumbers, melons, leeks, onions, and garlic.

verses.esv: But now our strength is dried up, and there is nothing at all but this manna to look at.”
verses.kjv: But now our soul is dried away: there is nothing at all, beside this manna, before our eyes.
verses.bsb: But now our appetite is gone; there is nothing to see but this manna!”

verses.esv: Now the manna was like coriander seed, and its appearance like that of bdellium.
verses.kjv: And the manna was as coriander seed, and the colour thereof as the colour of bdellium.
verses.bsb: Now the manna resembled coriander seed, and its appearance was like that of gum resin.

verses.esv: The people went about and gathered it and ground it in handmills or beat it in mortars and boiled it in pots and made cakes of it. And the taste of it was like the taste of cakes baked with oil.
verses.kjv: And the people went about, and gathered it, and ground it in mills, or beat it in a mortar, and baked it in pans, and made cakes of it: and the taste of it was as the taste of fresh oil.
verses.bsb: The people walked around and gathered it, ground it on a handmill or crushed it in a mortar, then boiled it in a cooking pot or shaped it into cakes. It tasted like pastry baked with fine oil.

verses.esv: When the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell with it.
verses.kjv: And when the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell upon it.
verses.bsb: When the dew fell on the camp at night, the manna would fall with it.

verses.esv: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their clans, everyone at the door of his tent. And the anger of the LORD blazed hotly, and Moses was displeased.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Moses heard the people weep throughout their families, every man in the door of his tent: and the anger of the Lord was kindled greatly; Moses also was displeased.
verses.bsb: Then Moses heard the people of family after family weeping at the entrances to their tents, and the anger of the LORD was kindled greatly, and Moses was also displeased.

verses.esv: Moses said to the LORD, “Why have you dealt ill with your servant? And why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?
verses.kjv: And Moses said unto the Lord, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?
verses.bsb: So Moses asked the LORD, “Why have You brought this trouble on Your servant? Why have I not found favor in Your sight, that You have laid upon me the burden of all these people?

verses.esv: Did I conceive all this people? Did I give them birth, that you should say to me, ‘Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries a nursing child,’ to the land that you swore to give their fathers?
verses.kjv: Have I conceived all this people? have I begotten them, that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father beareth the sucking child, unto the land which thou swarest unto their fathers?
verses.bsb: Did I conceive all these people? Did I give them birth, so that You should tell me, ‘Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries an infant,’ to the land that You swore to give their fathers?

verses.esv: Where am I to get meat to give to all this people? For they weep before me and say, ‘Give us meat, that we may eat.’
verses.kjv: Whence should I have flesh to give unto all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give us flesh, that we may eat.
verses.bsb: Where can I get meat for all these people? For they keep crying out to me, ‘Give us meat to eat!’

verses.esv: I am not able to carry all this people alone; the burden is too heavy for me.
verses.kjv: I am not able to bear all this people alone, because it is too heavy for me.
verses.bsb: I cannot carry all these people by myself; it is too burdensome for me.

verses.esv: If you will treat me like this, kill me at once, if I find favor in your sight, that I may not see my wretchedness.”
verses.kjv: And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.
verses.bsb: If this is how You are going to treat me, please kill me right now—if I have found favor in Your eyes—and let me not see my own wretchedness.”

verses.esv: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Gather for me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting, and let them take their stand there with you.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Bring Me seventy of the elders of Israel known to you as leaders and officers of the people. Bring them to the Tent of Meeting and have them stand there with you.

verses.esv: And I will come down and talk with you there. And I will take some of the Spirit that is on you and put it on them, and they shall bear the burden of the people with you, so that you may not bear it yourself alone.
verses.kjv: And I will come down and talk with thee there: and I will take of the spirit which is upon thee, and will put it upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, that thou bear it not thyself alone.
verses.bsb: And I will come down and speak with you there, and I will take some of the Spirit that is on you and put that Spirit on them. They will help you bear the burden of the people, so that you do not have to bear it by yourself.

verses.esv: And say to the people, ‘Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat meat, for you have wept in the hearing of the LORD, saying, “Who will give us meat to eat? For it was better for us in Egypt.” Therefore the LORD will give you meat, and you shall eat.
verses.kjv: And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the Lord, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt: therefore the Lord will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.
verses.bsb: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you will eat meat, because you have cried out in the hearing of the LORD, saying: ‘Who will feed us meat? For we were better off in Egypt!’ Therefore the LORD will give you meat, and you will eat.

verses.esv: You shall not eat just one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or twenty days,
verses.kjv: Ye shall not eat one day, nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, nor twenty days;
verses.bsb: You will eat it not for one or two days, nor for five or ten or twenty days,

verses.esv: but a whole month, until it comes out at your nostrils and becomes loathsome to you, because you have rejected the LORD who is among you and have wept before him, saying, “Why did we come out of Egypt?”’”
verses.kjv: But even a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it be loathsome unto you: because that ye have despised the Lord which is among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?
verses.bsb: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your nostrils and makes you nauseous—because you have rejected the LORD, who is among you, and have cried out before Him, saying, ‘Why did we ever leave Egypt?’”

verses.esv: But Moses said, “The people among whom I am number six hundred thousand on foot, and you have said, ‘I will give them meat, that they may eat a whole month!’
verses.kjv: And Moses said, The people, among whom I am, are six hundred thousand footmen; and thou hast said, I will give them flesh, that they may eat a whole month.
verses.bsb: But Moses replied, “Here I am among 600,000 men on foot, yet You say, ‘I will give them meat, and they will eat for a month.’

verses.esv: Shall flocks and herds be slaughtered for them, and be enough for them? Or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them, and be enough for them?”
verses.kjv: Shall the flocks and the herds be slain for them, to suffice them? or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them, to suffice them?
verses.bsb: If all our flocks and herds were slaughtered for them, would they have enough? Or if all the fish in the sea were caught for them, would they have enough?”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “Is the LORD's hand shortened? Now you shall see whether my word will come true for you or not.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Is the Lord’s hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.
verses.bsb: The LORD answered Moses, “Is the LORD’s arm too short? Now you will see whether or not My word will come to pass.”

verses.esv: So Moses went out and told the people the words of the LORD. And he gathered seventy men of the elders of the people and placed them around the tent.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses went out, and told the people the words of the Lord, and gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: So Moses went out and relayed to the people the words of the LORD, and he gathered seventy of the elders of the people and had them stand around the tent.

verses.esv: Then the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the Spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders. And as soon as the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied. But they did not continue doing it.
verses.kjv: And the Lord came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave it unto the seventy elders: and it came to pass, that, when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and He took some of the Spirit that was on Moses and placed that Spirit on the seventy elders. As the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied—but they never did so again.

verses.esv: Now two men remained in the camp, one named Eldad, and the other named Medad, and the Spirit rested on them. They were among those registered, but they had not gone out to the tent, and so they prophesied in the camp.
verses.kjv: But there remained two of the men in the camp, the name of the one was Eldad, and the name of the other Medad: and the spirit rested upon them; and they were of them that were written, but went not out unto the tabernacle: and they prophesied in the camp.
verses.bsb: Two men, however, had remained in the camp—one named Eldad and the other Medad—and the Spirit rested on them. They were among those listed, but they had not gone out to the tent, and they prophesied in the camp.

verses.esv: And a young man ran and told Moses, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.”
verses.kjv: And there ran a young man, and told Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the camp.
verses.bsb: A young man ran and reported to Moses, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.”

verses.esv: And Joshua the son of Nun, the assistant of Moses from his youth, said, “My lord Moses, stop them.”
verses.kjv: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Moses, one of his young men, answered and said, My lord Moses, forbid them.
verses.bsb: Joshua son of Nun, the attendant to Moses since youth, spoke up and said, “Moses, my lord, stop them!”

verses.esv: But Moses said to him, “Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all the LORD's people were prophets, that the LORD would put his Spirit on them!”
verses.kjv: And Moses said unto him, Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the Lord’s people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit upon them!
verses.bsb: But Moses replied, “Are you jealous on my account? I wish that all the LORD’s people were prophets and that the LORD would place His Spirit on them!”

verses.esv: And Moses and the elders of Israel returned to the camp.
verses.kjv: And Moses gat him into the camp, he and the elders of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then Moses returned to the camp, along with the elders of Israel.

verses.esv: Then a wind from the LORD sprang up, and it brought quail from the sea and let them fall beside the camp, about a day's journey on this side and a day's journey on the other side, around the camp, and about two cubits above the ground.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there went forth a wind from the Lord, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day’s journey on this side, and as it were a day’s journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: Now a wind sent by the LORD came up, drove in quail from the sea, and brought them near the camp, about two cubits above the surface of the ground, for a day’s journey in every direction around the camp.

verses.esv: And the people rose all that day and all night and all the next day, and gathered the quail. Those who gathered least gathered ten homers. And they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
verses.kjv: And the people stood up all that day, and all that night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: he that gathered least gathered ten homers: and they spread them all abroad for themselves round about the camp.
verses.bsb: All that day and night, and all the next day, the people stayed up gathering the quail. No one gathered less than ten homers, and they spread them out all around the camp.

verses.esv: While the meat was yet between their teeth, before it was consumed, the anger of the LORD was kindled against the people, and the LORD struck down the people with a very great plague.
verses.kjv: And while the flesh was yet between their teeth, ere it was chewed, the wrath of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord smote the people with a very great plague.
verses.bsb: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it was chewed, the anger of the LORD burned against the people, and the LORD struck them with a severe plague.

verses.esv: Therefore the name of that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had the craving.
verses.kjv: And he called the name of that place Kibroth-hattaavah: because there they buried the people that lusted.
verses.bsb: So they called that place Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had craved other food.

verses.esv: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people journeyed to Hazeroth, and they remained at Hazeroth.
verses.kjv: And the people journeyed from Kibroth-hattaavah unto Hazeroth; and abode at Hazeroth.
verses.bsb: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people moved on to Hazeroth, where they remained for some time.

verses.esv: Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because of the Cushite woman whom he had married, for he had married a Cushite woman.
verses.kjv: AND Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses because of the Ethiopian woman whom he had married: for he had married an Ethiopian woman.
verses.bsb: Then Miriam and Aaron criticized Moses because of the Cushite woman he had married, for he had taken a Cushite wife.

verses.esv: And they said, “Has the LORD indeed spoken only through Moses? Has he not spoken through us also?” And the LORD heard it.
verses.kjv: And they said, Hath the Lord indeed spoken only by Moses? hath he not spoken also by us? And the Lord heard it.
verses.bsb: “Does the LORD speak only through Moses?” they said. “Does He not also speak through us?” And the LORD heard this.

verses.esv: Now the man Moses was very meek, more than all people who were on the face of the earth.
verses.kjv: (Now the man Moses was very meek, above all the men which were upon the face of the earth.)
verses.bsb: Now Moses was a very humble man, more so than any man on the face of the earth.

verses.esv: And suddenly the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron and Miriam, “Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting.” And the three of them came out.
verses.kjv: And the Lord spake suddenly unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tabernacle of the congregation. And they three came out.
verses.bsb: And suddenly the LORD said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, “You three, come out to the Tent of Meeting.” So the three went out,

verses.esv: And the LORD came down in a pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance of the tent and called Aaron and Miriam, and they both came forward.
verses.kjv: And the Lord came down in the pillar of the cloud, and stood in the door of the tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam: and they both came forth.
verses.bsb: and the LORD came down in a pillar of cloud, stood at the entrance to the Tent, and summoned Aaron and Miriam. When both of them had stepped forward,

verses.esv: And he said, “Hear my words: If there is a prophet among you, I the LORD make myself known to him in a vision; I speak with him in a dream.
verses.kjv: And he said, Hear now my words: If there be a prophet among you, I the Lord will make myself known unto him in a vision, and will speak unto him in a dream.
verses.bsb: He said, “Hear now My words:  If there is a prophet among you, I, the LORD, will reveal Myself to him in a vision; I will speak to him in a dream.

verses.esv: Not so with my servant Moses. He is faithful in all my house.
verses.kjv: My servant Moses is not so, who is faithful in all mine house.
verses.bsb: But this is not so with My servant Moses; he is faithful in all My house.

verses.esv: With him I speak mouth to mouth, clearly, and not in riddles, and he beholds the form of the LORD. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?”
verses.kjv: With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches; and the similitude of the Lord shall he behold: wherefore then were ye not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?
verses.bsb: I speak with him face to face, clearly and not in riddles; he sees the form of the LORD.   Why then were you unafraid to speak against My servant Moses?”

verses.esv: And the anger of the LORD was kindled against them, and he departed.
verses.kjv: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them; and he departed.
verses.bsb: So the anger of the LORD burned against them, and He departed.

verses.esv: When the cloud removed from over the tent, behold, Miriam was leprous, like snow. And Aaron turned toward Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.
verses.kjv: And the cloud departed from off the tabernacle; and, behold, Miriam became leprous, white as snow: and Aaron looked upon Miriam, and, behold, she was leprous.
verses.bsb: As the cloud lifted from above the Tent, suddenly Miriam became leprous, white as snow. Aaron turned toward her, saw that she was leprous,

verses.esv: And Aaron said to Moses, “Oh, my lord, do not punish us because we have done foolishly and have sinned.
verses.kjv: And Aaron said unto Moses, Alas, my lord, I beseech thee, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly, and wherein we have sinned.
verses.bsb: and said to Moses, “My lord, please do not hold against us this sin we have so foolishly committed.

verses.esv: Let her not be as one dead, whose flesh is half eaten away when he comes out of his mother's womb.”
verses.kjv: Let her not be as one dead, of whom the flesh is half consumed when he cometh out of his mother’s womb.
verses.bsb: Please do not let her be like a stillborn infant whose flesh is half consumed when he comes out of his mother’s womb.”

verses.esv: And Moses cried to the LORD, “O God, please heal her—please.”
verses.kjv: And Moses cried unto the Lord, saying, Heal her now, O God, I beseech thee.
verses.bsb: So Moses cried out to the LORD, “O God, please heal her!”

verses.esv: But the LORD said to Moses, “If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be shamed seven days? Let her be shut outside the camp seven days, and after that she may be brought in again.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be ashamed seven days? let her be shut out from the camp seven days, and after that let her be received in again.
verses.bsb: But the LORD answered Moses, “If her father had but spit in her face, would she not have been in disgrace for seven days? Let her be confined outside the camp for seven days; after that she may be brought back in.”

verses.esv: So Miriam was shut outside the camp seven days, and the people did not set out on the march till Miriam was brought in again.
verses.kjv: And Miriam was shut out from the camp seven days: and the people journeyed not till Miriam was brought in again.
verses.bsb: So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days, and the people did not move on until she was brought in again.

verses.esv: After that the people set out from Hazeroth, and camped in the wilderness of Paran.
verses.kjv: And afterward the people removed from Hazeroth, and pitched in the wilderness of Paran.
verses.bsb: After that, the people set out from Hazeroth and camped in the Wilderness of Paran.

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Send men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the people of Israel. From each tribe of their fathers you shall send a man, every one a chief among them.”
verses.kjv: Send thou men, that they may search the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel: of every tribe of their fathers shall ye send a man, every one a ruler among them.
verses.bsb: “Send out for yourself men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites. From each of their fathers’ tribes send one man who is a leader among them.”

verses.esv: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, according to the command of the LORD, all of them men who were heads of the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Moses by the commandment of the Lord sent them from the wilderness of Paran: all those men were heads of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: So at the consent of the LORD, Moses sent them out from the Wilderness of Paran. All the men were leaders of the Israelites,

verses.esv: And these were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur;
verses.kjv: And these were their names: of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur.
verses.bsb: and these were their names:  From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Nun;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea the son of Nun.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti the son of Raphu;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti the son of Raphu.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Joseph (that is, from the tribe of Manasseh), Gaddi the son of Susi;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Joseph, namely, of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the son of Susi.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Manasseh (a tribe of Joseph), Gaddi son of Susi;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the son of Vophsi;
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the son of Vophsi.
verses.bsb: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;

verses.esv: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel the son of Machi.
verses.kjv: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel the son of Machi.
verses.bsb: and from the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.

verses.esv: These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Hoshea the son of Nun Joshua.
verses.kjv: These are the names of the men which Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Oshea the son of Nun Jehoshua.
verses.bsb: These were the names of the men Moses sent to spy out the land; and Moses gave to Hoshea son of Nun the name Joshua.

verses.esv: Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan and said to them, “Go up into the Negeb and go up into the hill country,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said unto them, Get you up this way southward, and go up into the mountain:
verses.bsb: When Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, he told them, “Go up through the Negev and into the hill country.

verses.esv: and see what the land is, and whether the people who dwell in it are strong or weak, whether they are few or many,
verses.kjv: And see the land, what it is; and the people that dwelleth therein, whether they be strong or weak, few or many;
verses.bsb: See what the land is like and whether its people are strong or weak, few or many.

verses.esv: and whether the land that they dwell in is good or bad, and whether the cities that they dwell in are camps or strongholds,
verses.kjv: And what the land is that they dwell in, whether it be good or bad; and what cities they be that they dwell in, whether in tents, or in strong holds;
verses.bsb: Is the land where they live good or bad? Are the cities where they dwell open camps or fortifications?

verses.esv: and whether the land is rich or poor, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be of good courage and bring some of the fruit of the land.” Now the time was the season of the first ripe grapes.
verses.kjv: And what the land is, whether it be fat or lean, whether there be wood therein, or not. And be ye of good courage, and bring of the fruit of the land. Now the time was the time of the firstripe grapes.
verses.bsb: Is the soil fertile or unproductive? Are there trees in it or not? Be courageous, and bring back some of the fruit of the land.” (It was the season for the first ripe grapes.)

verses.esv: So they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
verses.kjv: ¶ So they went up, and searched the land from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob, as men come to Hamath.
verses.bsb: So they went up and spied out the land from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob, toward Lebo-hamath.

verses.esv: They went up into the Negeb and came to Hebron. Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak, were there. (Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
verses.kjv: And they ascended by the south, and came unto Hebron; where Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak, were. (Now Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
verses.bsb: They went up through the Negev and came to Hebron, where Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak, dwelled. It had been built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.

verses.esv: And they came to the Valley of Eshcol and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them; they also brought some pomegranates and figs.
verses.kjv: And they came unto the brook of Eshcol, and cut down from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they bare it between two upon a staff; and they brought of the pomegranates, and of the figs.
verses.bsb: When they came to the Valley of Eshcol, they cut down a branch with a single cluster of grapes, which they carried on a pole between two men. They also took some pomegranates and figs.

verses.esv: That place was called the Valley of Eshcol, because of the cluster that the people of Israel cut down from there.
verses.kjv: The place was called the brook Eshcol, because of the cluster of grapes which the children of Israel cut down from thence.
verses.bsb: Because of the cluster of grapes the Israelites cut there, that place was called the Valley of Eshcol.

verses.esv: At the end of forty days they returned from spying out the land.
verses.kjv: And they returned from searching of the land after forty days.
verses.bsb: After forty days the men returned from spying out the land,

verses.esv: And they came to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation of the people of Israel in the wilderness of Paran, at Kadesh. They brought back word to them and to all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they went and came to Moses, and to Aaron, and to all the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and brought back word unto them, and unto all the congregation, and shewed them the fruit of the land.
verses.bsb: and they went back to Moses, Aaron, and the whole congregation of Israel in the Wilderness of Paran at Kadesh. They brought back a report for the whole congregation and showed them the fruit of the land.

verses.esv: And they told him, “We came to the land to which you sent us. It flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
verses.kjv: And they told him, and said, We came unto the land whither thou sentest us, and surely it floweth with milk and honey; and this is the fruit of it.
verses.bsb: And they gave this account to Moses: “We went into the land to which you sent us, and indeed, it is flowing with milk and honey. Here is some of its fruit!

verses.esv: However, the people who dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified and very large. And besides, we saw the descendants of Anak there.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the people be strong that dwell in the land, and the cities are walled, and very great: and moreover we saw the children of Anak there.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, the people living in the land are strong, and the cities are large and fortified. We even saw the descendants of Anak there.

verses.esv: The Amalekites dwell in the land of the Negeb. The Hittites, the Jebusites, and the Amorites dwell in the hill country. And the Canaanites dwell by the sea, and along the Jordan.”
verses.kjv: The Amalekites dwell in the land of the south: and the Hittites, and the Jebusites, and the Amorites, dwell in the mountains: and the Canaanites dwell by the sea, and by the coast of Jordan.
verses.bsb: The Amalekites live in the land of the Negev; the Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country; and the Canaanites live by the sea and along the Jordan.”

verses.esv: But Caleb quieted the people before Moses and said, “Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it.”
verses.kjv: And Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, Let us go up at once, and possess it; for we are well able to overcome it.
verses.bsb: Then Caleb quieted the people before Moses and said, “We must go up and take possession of the land, for we can certainly conquer it!”

verses.esv: Then the men who had gone up with him said, “We are not able to go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are.”
verses.kjv: But the men that went up with him said, We be not able to go up against the people; for they are stronger than we.
verses.bsb: But the men who had gone up with him replied, “We cannot go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are!”

verses.esv: So they brought to the people of Israel a bad report of the land that they had spied out, saying, “The land, through which we have gone to spy it out, is a land that devours its inhabitants, and all the people that we saw in it are of great height.
verses.kjv: And they brought up an evil report of the land which they had searched unto the children of Israel, saying, The land, through which we have gone to search it, is a land that eateth up the inhabitants thereof; and all the people that we saw in it are men of a great stature.
verses.bsb: So they gave the Israelites a bad report about the land that they had spied out: “The land we explored devours its inhabitants, and all the people we saw there are great in stature.

verses.esv: And there we saw the Nephilim (the sons of Anak, who come from the Nephilim), and we seemed to ourselves like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them.”
verses.kjv: And there we saw the giants, the sons of Anak, which come of the giants: and we were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so we were in their sight.
verses.bsb: We even saw the Nephilim there—the descendants of Anak that come from the Nephilim! We seemed like grasshoppers in our own sight, and we must have seemed the same to them!”

verses.esv: Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.
verses.kjv: AND all the congregation lifted up their voice, and cried; and the people wept that night.
verses.bsb: Then the whole congregation lifted up their voices and cried out, and that night the people wept.

verses.esv: And all the people of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron. The whole congregation said to them, “Would that we had died in the land of Egypt! Or would that we had died in this wilderness!
verses.kjv: And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron: and the whole congregation said unto them, Would God that we had died in the land of Egypt! or would God we had died in this wilderness!
verses.bsb: All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron, and the whole congregation said to them, “If only we had died in the land of Egypt, or if only we had died in this wilderness!

verses.esv: Why is the LORD bringing us into this land, to fall by the sword? Our wives and our little ones will become a prey. Would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?”
verses.kjv: And wherefore hath the Lord brought us unto this land, to fall by the sword, that our wives and our children should be a prey? were it not better for us to return into Egypt?
verses.bsb: Why is the LORD bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and children will become plunder. Would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?”

verses.esv: And they said to one another, “Let us choose a leader and go back to Egypt.”
verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Let us make a captain, and let us return into Egypt.
verses.bsb: So they said to one another, “Let us appoint a leader and return to Egypt.”

verses.esv: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then Moses and Aaron fell facedown before the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel.

verses.esv: And Joshua the son of Nun and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, which were of them that searched the land, rent their clothes:
verses.bsb: Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes

verses.esv: and said to all the congregation of the people of Israel, “The land, which we passed through to spy it out, is an exceedingly good land.
verses.kjv: And they spake unto all the company of the children of Israel, saying, The land, which we passed through to search it, is an exceeding good land.
verses.bsb: and said to the whole congregation of Israel, “The land we passed through and explored is an exceedingly good land.

verses.esv: If the LORD delights in us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
verses.kjv: If the Lord delight in us, then he will bring us into this land, and give it us; a land which floweth with milk and honey.
verses.bsb: If the LORD delights in us, He will bring us into this land, a land flowing with milk and honey, and He will give it to us.

verses.esv: Only do not rebel against the LORD. And do not fear the people of the land, for they are bread for us. Their protection is removed from them, and the LORD is with us; do not fear them.”
verses.kjv: Only rebel not ye against the Lord, neither fear ye the people of the land; for they are bread for us: their defence is departed from them, and the Lord is with us: fear them not.
verses.bsb: Only do not rebel against the LORD, and do not be afraid of the people of the land, for they will be like bread for us. Their protection has been removed, and the LORD is with us. Do not be afraid of them!”

verses.esv: Then all the congregation said to stone them with stones. But the glory of the LORD appeared at the tent of meeting to all the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: But all the congregation bade stone them with stones. And the glory of the Lord appeared in the tabernacle of the congregation before all the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: But the whole congregation threatened to stone Joshua and Caleb.  Then the glory of the LORD appeared to all the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “How long will this people despise me? And how long will they not believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, How long will this people provoke me? and how long will it be ere they believe me, for all the signs which I have shewed among them?
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “How long will this people treat Me with contempt? How long will they refuse to believe in Me, despite all the signs I have performed among them?

verses.esv: I will strike them with the pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they.”
verses.kjv: I will smite them with the pestilence, and disinherit them, and will make of thee a greater nation and mightier than they.
verses.bsb: I will strike them with a plague and destroy them—and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than they are.”

verses.esv: But Moses said to the LORD, “Then the Egyptians will hear of it, for you brought up this people in your might from among them,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the Lord, Then the Egyptians shall hear it, (for thou broughtest up this people in thy might from among them;)
verses.bsb: But Moses said to the LORD, “The Egyptians will hear of it, for by Your strength You brought this people from among them.

verses.esv: and they will tell the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you, O LORD, are in the midst of this people. For you, O LORD, are seen face to face, and your cloud stands over them and you go before them, in a pillar of cloud by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
verses.kjv: And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land: for they have heard that thou Lord art among this people, that thou Lord art seen face to face, and that thy cloud standeth over them, and that thou goest before them, by day time in a pillar of a cloud, and in a pillar of fire by night.
verses.bsb: And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land. They have already heard that You, O LORD, are in the midst of this people, that You, O LORD, have been seen face to face, that Your cloud stands over them, and that You go before them in a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.

verses.esv: Now if you kill this people as one man, then the nations who have heard your fame will say,
verses.kjv: ¶ Now if thou shalt kill all this people as one man, then the nations which have heard the fame of thee will speak, saying,
verses.bsb: If You kill this people as one man, the nations who have heard of Your fame will say,

verses.esv: ‘It is because the LORD was not able to bring this people into the land that he swore to give to them that he has killed them in the wilderness.’
verses.kjv: Because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land which he sware unto them, therefore he hath slain them in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: ‘Because the LORD was unable to bring this people into the land He swore to give them, He has slaughtered them in the wilderness.’

verses.esv: And now, please let the power of the Lord be great as you have promised, saying,
verses.kjv: And now, I beseech thee, let the power of my Lord be great, according as thou hast spoken, saying,
verses.bsb: So now I pray, may the power of my Lord be magnified, just as You have declared:

verses.esv: ‘The LORD is slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but he will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children, to the third and the fourth generation.’
verses.kjv: The Lord is longsuffering, and of great mercy, forgiving iniquity and transgression, and by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation.
verses.bsb: ‘The LORD is slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion, forgiving iniquity and transgression. Yet He will by no means leave the guilty unpunished; He will visit the iniquity of the fathers upon their children to the third and fourth generation.’

verses.esv: Please pardon the iniquity of this people, according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have forgiven this people, from Egypt until now.”
verses.kjv: Pardon, I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people according unto the greatness of thy mercy, and as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even until now.
verses.bsb: Pardon, I pray, the iniquity of this people, in keeping with the greatness of Your loving devotion, just as You have forgiven them ever since they left Egypt.”

verses.esv: Then the LORD said, “I have pardoned, according to your word.
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, I have pardoned according to thy word:
verses.bsb: “I have pardoned them as you requested,” the LORD replied.

verses.esv: But truly, as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the LORD,
verses.kjv: But as truly as I live, all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Yet as surely as I live and as surely as the whole earth is filled with the glory of the LORD,

verses.esv: none of the men who have seen my glory and my signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have put me to the test these ten times and have not obeyed my voice,
verses.kjv: Because all those men which have seen my glory, and my miracles, which I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have tempted me now these ten times, and have not hearkened to my voice;
verses.bsb: not one of the men who have seen My glory and the signs I performed in Egypt and in the wilderness—yet have tested Me and disobeyed Me these ten times—

verses.esv: shall see the land that I swore to give to their fathers. And none of those who despised me shall see it.
verses.kjv: Surely they shall not see the land which I sware unto their fathers, neither shall any of them that provoked me see it:
verses.bsb: not one will ever see the land that I swore to give their fathers. None of those who have treated Me with contempt will see it.

verses.esv: But my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and has followed me fully, I will bring into the land into which he went, and his descendants shall possess it.
verses.kjv: But my servant Caleb, because he had another spirit with him, and hath followed me fully, him will I bring into the land whereinto he went; and his seed shall possess it.
verses.bsb: But because My servant Caleb has a different spirit and has followed Me wholeheartedly, I will bring him into the land he has entered, and his descendants will inherit it.

verses.esv: Now, since the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwell in the valleys, turn tomorrow and set out for the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea.”
verses.kjv: (Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwelt in the valley.) To morrow turn you, and get you into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.
verses.bsb: Now since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the valleys, turn back tomorrow and head for the wilderness along the route to the Red Sea.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,

verses.esv: “How long shall this wicked congregation grumble against me? I have heard the grumblings of the people of Israel, which they grumble against me.
verses.kjv: How long shall I bear with this evil congregation, which murmur against me? I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmur against me.
verses.bsb: “How long will this wicked congregation grumble against Me? I have heard the complaints that the Israelites are making against Me.

verses.esv: Say to them, ‘As I live, declares the LORD, what you have said in my hearing I will do to you:
verses.kjv: Say unto them, As truly as I live, saith the Lord, as ye have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you:
verses.bsb: So tell them: As surely as I live, declares the LORD, I will do to you exactly as I heard you say.

verses.esv: your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness, and of all your number, listed in the census from twenty years old and upward, who have grumbled against me,
verses.kjv: Your carcases shall fall in this wilderness; and all that were numbered of you, according to your whole number, from twenty years old and upward, which have murmured against me,
verses.bsb: Your bodies will fall in this wilderness—all who were numbered in the census, everyone twenty years of age or older—because you have grumbled against Me.

verses.esv: not one shall come into the land where I swore that I would make you dwell, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.kjv: Doubtless ye shall not come into the land, concerning which I sware to make you dwell therein, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.bsb: Surely none of you will enter the land in which I swore to settle you, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

verses.esv: But your little ones, who you said would become a prey, I will bring in, and they shall know the land that you have rejected.
verses.kjv: But your little ones, which ye said should be a prey, them will I bring in, and they shall know the land which ye have despised.
verses.bsb: But I will bring your children, whom you said would become plunder, into the land you have rejected—and they will enjoy it.

verses.esv: But as for you, your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.
verses.kjv: But as for you, your carcases, they shall fall in this wilderness.
verses.bsb: As for you, however, your bodies will fall in this wilderness.

verses.esv: And your children shall be shepherds in the wilderness forty years and shall suffer for your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.
verses.kjv: And your children shall wander in the wilderness forty years, and bear your whoredoms, until your carcases be wasted in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: Your children will be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and they will suffer for your unfaithfulness until the last of your bodies lies in the wilderness.

verses.esv: According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land, forty days, a year for each day, you shall bear your iniquity forty years, and you shall know my displeasure.’
verses.kjv: After the number of the days in which ye searched the land, even forty days, each day for a year, shall ye bear your iniquities, even forty years, and ye shall know my breach of promise.
verses.bsb: In keeping with the forty days you spied out the land, you shall bear your guilt forty years—a year for each day—and you will experience My alienation.

verses.esv: I, the LORD, have spoken. Surely this will I do to all this wicked congregation who are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall come to a full end, and there they shall die.”
verses.kjv: I the Lord have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
verses.bsb: I, the LORD, have spoken, and I will surely do these things to this entire wicked congregation, which has conspired against Me. They will meet their end in the wilderness, and there they will die.”

verses.esv: And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, who returned and made all the congregation grumble against him by bringing up a bad report about the land—
verses.kjv: And the men, which Moses sent to search the land, who returned, and made all the congregation to murmur against him, by bringing up a slander upon the land,
verses.bsb: So the men Moses had sent to spy out the land, who had returned and made the whole congregation grumble against him by bringing out a bad report about the land—

verses.esv: the men who brought up a bad report of the land—died by plague before the LORD.
verses.kjv: Even those men that did bring up the evil report upon the land, died by the plague before the Lord.
verses.bsb: those men who had brought out the bad report about the land—were struck down by a plague before the LORD.

verses.esv: Of those men who went to spy out the land, only Joshua the son of Nun and Caleb the son of Jephunneh remained alive.
verses.kjv: But Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, which were of the men that went to search the land, lived still.
verses.bsb: Of those men who had gone to spy out the land, only Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh remained alive.

verses.esv: When Moses told these words to all the people of Israel, the people mourned greatly.
verses.kjv: And Moses told these sayings unto all the children of Israel: and the people mourned greatly.
verses.bsb: And when Moses relayed these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned bitterly.

verses.esv: And they rose early in the morning and went up to the heights of the hill country, saying, “Here we are. We will go up to the place that the LORD has promised, for we have sinned.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And they rose up early in the morning, and gat them up into the top of the mountain, saying, Lo, we be here, and will go up unto the place which the Lord hath promised: for we have sinned.
verses.bsb: Early the next morning they got up and went up toward the ridge of the hill country. “We have indeed sinned,” they said, “but we will go to the place the LORD has promised.”

verses.esv: But Moses said, “Why now are you transgressing the command of the LORD, when that will not succeed?
verses.kjv: And Moses said, Wherefore now do ye transgress the commandment of the Lord? but it shall not prosper.
verses.bsb: But Moses said, “Why are you transgressing the commandment of the LORD? This will not succeed!

verses.esv: Do not go up, for the LORD is not among you, lest you be struck down before your enemies.
verses.kjv: Go not up, for the Lord is not among you; that ye be not smitten before your enemies.
verses.bsb: Do not go up, lest you be struck down by your enemies, because the LORD is not among you.

verses.esv: For there the Amalekites and the Canaanites are facing you, and you shall fall by the sword. Because you have turned back from following the LORD, the LORD will not be with you.”
verses.kjv: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites are there before you, and ye shall fall by the sword: because ye are turned away from the Lord, therefore the Lord will not be with you.
verses.bsb: For there the Amalekites and Canaanites will face you, and you will fall by the sword. Because you have turned away from the LORD, He will not be with you.”

verses.esv: But they presumed to go up to the heights of the hill country, although neither the ark of the covenant of the LORD nor Moses departed out of the camp.
verses.kjv: But they presumed to go up unto the hill top: nevertheless the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and Moses, departed not out of the camp.
verses.bsb: But they dared to go up to the ridge of the hill country, though neither Moses nor the ark of the covenant of the LORD moved from the camp.

verses.esv: Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down and defeated them and pursued them, even to Hormah.
verses.kjv: Then the Amalekites came down, and the Canaanites which dwelt in that hill, and smote them, and discomfited them, even unto Hormah.
verses.bsb: Then the Amalekites and Canaanites who lived in that part of the hill country came down, attacked them, and routed them all the way to Hormah.

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, When you come into the land you are to inhabit, which I am giving you,
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land of your habitations, which I give unto you,
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: After you enter the land that I am giving you as a home

verses.esv: and you offer to the LORD from the herd or from the flock a food offering or a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering or at your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to the LORD,
verses.kjv: And will make an offering by fire unto the Lord, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts, to make a sweet savour unto the Lord, of the herd, or of the flock:
verses.bsb: and you present an offering made by fire to the LORD from the herd or flock to produce a pleasing aroma to the LORD—either a burnt offering or a sacrifice, for a special vow or freewill offering or appointed feast—

verses.esv: then he who brings his offering shall offer to the LORD a grain offering of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with a quarter of a hin of oil;
verses.kjv: Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the Lord bring a meat offering of a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of oil.
verses.bsb: then the one presenting his offering to the LORD shall also present a grain offering of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a quarter hin of olive oil.

verses.esv: and you shall offer with the burnt offering, or for the sacrifice, a quarter of a hin of wine for the drink offering for each lamb.
verses.kjv: And the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering shalt thou prepare with the burnt offering or sacrifice, for one lamb.
verses.bsb: With the burnt offering or sacrifice of each lamb, you are to prepare a quarter hin of wine as a drink offering.

verses.esv: Or for a ram, you shall offer for a grain offering two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of oil.
verses.kjv: Or for a ram, thou shalt prepare for a meat offering two tenth deals of flour mingled with the third part of an hin of oil.
verses.bsb: With a ram you are to prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of olive oil,

verses.esv: And for the drink offering you shall offer a third of a hin of wine, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
verses.kjv: And for a drink offering thou shalt offer the third part of an hin of wine, for a sweet savour unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: and a third of a hin of wine as a drink offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

verses.esv: And when you offer a bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or for peace offerings to the LORD,
verses.kjv: And when thou preparest a bullock for a burnt offering, or for a sacrifice in performing a vow, or peace offerings unto the Lord:
verses.bsb: When you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice to fulfill a vow or as a peace offering to the LORD,

verses.esv: then one shall offer with the bull a grain offering of three tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with half a hin of oil.
verses.kjv: Then shall he bring with a bullock a meat offering of three tenth deals of flour mingled with half an hin of oil.
verses.bsb: present with the bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of olive oil.

verses.esv: And you shall offer for the drink offering half a hin of wine, as a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring for a drink offering half an hin of wine, for an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Also present half a hin of wine as a drink offering. It is an offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

verses.esv: “Thus it shall be done for each bull or ram, or for each lamb or young goat.
verses.kjv: Thus shall it be done for one bullock, or for one ram, or for a lamb, or a kid.
verses.bsb: This is to be done for each bull, ram, lamb, or goat.

verses.esv: As many as you offer, so shall you do with each one, as many as there are.
verses.kjv: According to the number that ye shall prepare, so shall ye do to every one according to their number.
verses.bsb: This is how you must prepare each one, no matter how many.

verses.esv: Every native Israelite shall do these things in this way, in offering a food offering, with a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
verses.kjv: All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner, in offering an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Everyone who is native-born shall prepare these things in this way when he presents an offering made by fire as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

verses.esv: And if a stranger is sojourning with you, or anyone is living permanently among you, and he wishes to offer a food offering, with a pleasing aroma to the LORD, he shall do as you do.
verses.kjv: And if a stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever be among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord; as ye do, so he shall do.
verses.bsb: And for the generations to come, if a foreigner residing with you or someone else among you wants to prepare an offering made by fire as a pleasing aroma to the LORD, he is to do exactly as you do.

verses.esv: For the assembly, there shall be one statute for you and for the stranger who sojourns with you, a statute forever throughout your generations. You and the sojourner shall be alike before the LORD.
verses.kjv: One ordinance shall be both for you of the congregation, and also for the stranger that sojourneth with you, an ordinance for ever in your generations: as ye are, so shall the stranger be before the Lord.
verses.bsb: The assembly is to have the same statute both for you and for the foreign resident; it is a permanent statute for the generations to come. You and the foreigner shall be the same before the LORD.

verses.esv: One law and one rule shall be for you and for the stranger who sojourns with you.”
verses.kjv: One law and one manner shall be for you, and for the stranger that sojourneth with you.
verses.bsb: The same law and the same ordinance will apply both to you and to the foreigner residing with you.”

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, When you come into the land to which I bring you
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land whither I bring you,
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land to which I am bringing you

verses.esv: and when you eat of the bread of the land, you shall present a contribution to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Then it shall be, that, when ye eat of the bread of the land, ye shall offer up an heave offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: and you eat the food of the land, you shall lift up an offering to the LORD.

verses.esv: Of the first of your dough you shall present a loaf as a contribution; like a contribution from the threshing floor, so shall you present it.
verses.kjv: Ye shall offer up a cake of the first of your dough for an heave offering: as ye do the heave offering of the threshingfloor, so shall ye heave it.
verses.bsb: From the first of your dough, you are to lift up a cake as a contribution; offer it just like an offering from the threshing floor.

verses.esv: Some of the first of your dough you shall give to the LORD as a contribution throughout your generations.
verses.kjv: Of the first of your dough ye shall give unto the Lord an heave offering in your generations.
verses.bsb: Throughout your generations, you are to give the LORD an offering from the first of your dough.

verses.esv: “But if you sin unintentionally, and do not observe all these commandments that the LORD has spoken to Moses,
verses.kjv: ¶ And if ye have erred, and not observed all these commandments, which the Lord hath spoken unto Moses,
verses.bsb: Now if you stray unintentionally and do not obey all these commandments that the LORD has spoken to Moses—

verses.esv: all that the LORD has commanded you by Moses, from the day that the LORD gave commandment, and onward throughout your generations,
verses.kjv: Even all that the Lord hath commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that the Lord commanded Moses, and henceforward among your generations;
verses.bsb: all that the LORD has commanded you through Moses from the day the LORD gave them and continuing through the generations to come—

verses.esv: then if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation, all the congregation shall offer one bull from the herd for a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD, with its grain offering and its drink offering, according to the rule, and one male goat for a sin offering.
verses.kjv: Then it shall be, if ought be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation, that all the congregation shall offer one young bullock for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour unto the Lord, with his meat offering, and his drink offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.
verses.bsb: and if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation, then the whole congregation is to prepare one young bull as a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD, with its grain offering and drink offering according to the regulation, and one male goat as a sin offering.

verses.esv: And the priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the people of Israel, and they shall be forgiven, because it was a mistake, and they have brought their offering, a food offering to the LORD, and their sin offering before the LORD for their mistake.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall make an atonement for all the congregation of the children of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them; for it is ignorance: and they shall bring their offering, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord, and their sin offering before the Lord, for their ignorance:
verses.bsb: The priest is to make atonement for the whole congregation of Israel, so that they may be forgiven; for the sin was unintentional and they have brought to the LORD an offering made by fire and a sin offering, presented before the LORD for their unintentional sin.

verses.esv: And all the congregation of the people of Israel shall be forgiven, and the stranger who sojourns among them, because the whole population was involved in the mistake.
verses.kjv: And it shall be forgiven all the congregation of the children of Israel, and the stranger that sojourneth among them; seeing all the people were in ignorance.
verses.bsb: Then the whole congregation of Israel and the foreigners residing among them will be forgiven, since it happened to all the people unintentionally.

verses.esv: “If one person sins unintentionally, he shall offer a female goat a year old for a sin offering.
verses.kjv: ¶ And if any soul sin through ignorance, then he shall bring a she goat of the first year for a sin offering.
verses.bsb: Also, if one person sins unintentionally, he is to present a year-old female goat as a sin offering.

verses.esv: And the priest shall make atonement before the LORD for the person who makes a mistake, when he sins unintentionally, to make atonement for him, and he shall be forgiven.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the Lord, to make an atonement for him; and it shall be forgiven him.
verses.bsb: And the priest shall make atonement before the LORD on behalf of the person who erred by sinning unintentionally; and when atonement has been made for him, he will be forgiven.

verses.esv: You shall have one law for him who does anything unintentionally, for him who is native among the people of Israel and for the stranger who sojourns among them.
verses.kjv: Ye shall have one law for him that sinneth through ignorance, both for him that is born among the children of Israel, and for the stranger that sojourneth among them.
verses.bsb: You shall have the same law for the one who acts in error, whether he is a native-born Israelite or a foreigner residing among you.

verses.esv: But the person who does anything with a high hand, whether he is native or a sojourner, reviles the LORD, and that person shall be cut off from among his people.
verses.kjv: ¶ But the soul that doeth ought presumptuously, whether he be born in the land, or a stranger, the same reproacheth the Lord; and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
verses.bsb: But the person who sins defiantly, whether a native or foreigner, blasphemes the LORD. That person shall be cut off from among his people.

verses.esv: Because he has despised the word of the LORD and has broken his commandment, that person shall be utterly cut off; his iniquity shall be on him.”
verses.kjv: Because he hath despised the word of the Lord, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity shall be upon him.
verses.bsb: He shall certainly be cut off, because he has despised the word of the LORD and broken His commandment; his guilt remains on him.”

verses.esv: While the people of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering sticks on the Sabbath day.
verses.kjv: ¶ And while the children of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man that gathered sticks upon the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: While the Israelites were in the wilderness, a man was found gathering wood on the Sabbath day.

verses.esv: And those who found him gathering sticks brought him to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation.
verses.kjv: And they that found him gathering sticks brought him unto Moses and Aaron, and unto all the congregation.
verses.bsb: Those who found the man gathering wood brought him to Moses, Aaron, and the whole congregation,

verses.esv: They put him in custody, because it had not been made clear what should be done to him.
verses.kjv: And they put him in ward, because it was not declared what should be done to him.
verses.bsb: and because it had not been declared what should be done to him, they placed him in custody.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “The man shall be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, The man shall be surely put to death: all the congregation shall stone him with stones without the camp.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “The man must surely be put to death. The whole congregation is to stone him outside the camp.”

verses.esv: And all the congregation brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death with stones, as the LORD commanded Moses.
verses.kjv: And all the congregation brought him without the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died; as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: So the whole congregation took the man outside the camp and stoned him to death, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.esv: The LORD said to Moses,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Later, the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel, and tell them to make tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and to put a cord of blue on the tassel of each corner.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and bid them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments throughout their generations, and that they put upon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blue:
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout the generations to come they are to make for themselves tassels for the corners of their garments, with a blue cord on each tassel.

verses.esv: And it shall be a tassel for you to look at and remember all the commandments of the LORD, to do them, not to follow after your own heart and your own eyes, which you are inclined to whore after.
verses.kjv: And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the Lord, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:
verses.bsb: These will serve as tassels for you to look at, so that you may remember all the commandments of the LORD, that you may obey them and not prostitute yourselves by following your own heart and your own eyes.

verses.esv: So you shall remember and do all my commandments, and be holy to your God.
verses.kjv: That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.
verses.bsb: Then you will remember and obey all My commandments, and you will be holy to your God.

verses.esv: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD your God.”
verses.kjv: I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD your God.”

verses.esv: Now Korah the son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram the sons of Eliab, and On the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took men.
verses.kjv: NOW Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took men:
verses.bsb: Now Korah son of Izhar, the son of Kohath son of Levi, along with some Reubenites—Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth—conducted

verses.esv: And they rose up before Moses, with a number of the people of Israel, 250 chiefs of the congregation, chosen from the assembly, well-known men.
verses.kjv: And they rose up before Moses, with certain of the children of Israel, two hundred and fifty princes of the assembly, famous in the congregation, men of renown:
verses.bsb: a rebellion against Moses, along with 250 men of Israel renowned as leaders of the congregation and representatives in the assembly.

verses.esv: They assembled themselves together against Moses and against Aaron and said to them, “You have gone too far! For all in the congregation are holy, every one of them, and the LORD is among them. Why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of the LORD?”
verses.kjv: And they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron, and said unto them, Ye take too much upon you, seeing all the congregation are holy, every one of them, and the Lord is among them: wherefore then lift ye up yourselves above the congregation of the Lord?
verses.bsb: They came together against Moses and Aaron and told them, “You have taken too much upon yourselves! For everyone in the entire congregation is holy, and the LORD is in their midst. Why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of the LORD?”

verses.esv: When Moses heard it, he fell on his face,
verses.kjv: And when Moses heard it, he fell upon his face:
verses.bsb: When Moses heard this, he fell facedown.

verses.esv: and he said to Korah and all his company, “In the morning the LORD will show who is his, and who is holy, and will bring him near to him. The one whom he chooses he will bring near to him.
verses.kjv: And he spake unto Korah and unto all his company, saying, Even to morrow the Lord will shew who are his, and who is holy; and will cause him to come near unto him: even him whom he hath chosen will he cause to come near unto him.
verses.bsb: Then he said to Korah and all his followers, “Tomorrow morning the LORD will reveal who belongs to Him and who is holy, and He will bring that person near to Himself. The one He chooses, He will bring near to Himself.

verses.esv: Do this: take censers, Korah and all his company;
verses.kjv: This do; Take you censers, Korah, and all his company;
verses.bsb: You, Korah, and all your followers are to do as follows: Take censers,

verses.esv: put fire in them and put incense on them before the LORD tomorrow, and the man whom the LORD chooses shall be the holy one. You have gone too far, sons of Levi!”
verses.kjv: And put fire therein, and put incense in them before the Lord to morrow: and it shall be that the man whom the Lord doth choose, he shall be holy: ye take too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.
verses.bsb: and tomorrow you are to place fire and incense in them in the presence of the LORD. Then the man the LORD chooses will be the one who is holy. It is you sons of Levi who have taken too much upon yourselves!”

verses.esv: And Moses said to Korah, “Hear now, you sons of Levi:
verses.kjv: And Moses said unto Korah, Hear, I pray you, ye sons of Levi:
verses.bsb: Moses also said to Korah, “Now listen, you sons of Levi!

verses.esv: is it too small a thing for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself, to do service in the tabernacle of the LORD and to stand before the congregation to minister to them,
verses.kjv: Seemeth it but a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself to do the service of the tabernacle of the Lord, and to stand before the congregation to minister unto them?
verses.bsb: Is it not enough for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel and brought you near to Himself to perform the work at the LORD’s tabernacle, and to stand before the congregation to minister to them?

verses.esv: and that he has brought you near him, and all your brothers the sons of Levi with you? And would you seek the priesthood also?
verses.kjv: And he hath brought thee near to him, and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee: and seek ye the priesthood also?
verses.bsb: He has brought you near, you and all your fellow Levites, but you are seeking the priesthood as well.

verses.esv: Therefore it is against the LORD that you and all your company have gathered together. What is Aaron that you grumble against him?”
verses.kjv: For which cause both thou and all thy company are gathered together against the Lord: and what is Aaron, that ye murmur against him?
verses.bsb: Therefore, it is you and all your followers who have conspired against the LORD! As for Aaron, who is he that you should grumble against him?”

verses.esv: And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram the sons of Eliab, and they said, “We will not come up.
