verses.bsb: Then the Israelites traveled on and camped in the plains of Moab near the Jordan, across from Jericho.

verses.esv: And Balak the son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Balak the son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
verses.bsb: Now Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites,

verses.esv: And Moab was in great dread of the people, because they were many. Moab was overcome with fear of the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Moab was sore afraid of the people, because they were many: and Moab was distressed because of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: and Moab was terrified of the people because they were numerous. Indeed, Moab dreaded the Israelites.

verses.esv: And Moab said to the elders of Midian, “This horde will now lick up all that is around us, as the ox licks up the grass of the field.” So Balak the son of Zippor, who was king of Moab at that time,
verses.kjv: And Moab said unto the elders of Midian, Now shall this company lick up all that are round about us, as the ox licketh up the grass of the field. And Balak the son of Zippor was king of the Moabites at that time.
verses.bsb: So the Moabites said to the elders of Midian, “This horde will devour everything around us, as an ox licks up the grass of the field.”  Since Balak son of Zippor was king of Moab at that time,

verses.esv: sent messengers to Balaam the son of Beor at Pethor, which is near the River in the land of the people of Amaw, to call him, saying, “Behold, a people has come out of Egypt. They cover the face of the earth, and they are dwelling opposite me.
verses.kjv: He sent messengers therefore unto Balaam the son of Beor to Pethor, which is by the river of the land of the children of his people, to call him, saying, Behold, there is a people come out from Egypt: behold, they cover the face of the earth, and they abide over against me:
verses.bsb: he sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor, which is by the Euphrates in the land of his people.  “Behold, a people has come out of Egypt,” said Balak. “They cover the face of the land and have settled next to me.

verses.esv: Come now, curse this people for me, since they are too mighty for me. Perhaps I shall be able to defeat them and drive them from the land, for I know that he whom you bless is blessed, and he whom you curse is cursed.”
verses.kjv: Come now therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people; for they are too mighty for me: peradventure I shall prevail, that we may smite them, and that I may drive them out of the land: for I wot that he whom thou blessest is blessed, and he whom thou cursest is cursed.
verses.bsb: So please come now and put a curse on this people, because they are too mighty for me. Perhaps I may be able to defeat them and drive them out of the land; for I know that those you bless are blessed, and those you curse are cursed.”

verses.esv: So the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed with the fees for divination in their hand. And they came to Balaam and gave him Balak's message.
verses.kjv: And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed with the rewards of divination in their hand; and they came unto Balaam, and spake unto him the words of Balak.
verses.bsb: The elders of Moab and Midian departed with the fees for divination in hand. They came to Balaam and relayed to him the words of Balak.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Lodge here tonight, and I will bring back word to you, as the LORD speaks to me.” So the princes of Moab stayed with Balaam.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Lodge here this night, and I will bring you word again, as the Lord shall speak unto me: and the princes of Moab abode with Balaam.
verses.bsb: “Spend the night here,” Balaam replied, “and I will give you the answer that the LORD speaks to me.” So the princes of Moab stayed with Balaam.

verses.esv: And God came to Balaam and said, “Who are these men with you?”
verses.kjv: And God came unto Balaam, and said, What men are these with thee?
verses.bsb: Then God came to Balaam and asked, “Who are these men with you?”

verses.esv: And Balaam said to God, “Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab, has sent to me, saying,
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto God, Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab, hath sent unto me, saying,
verses.bsb: And Balaam said to God, “Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent me this message:

verses.esv: ‘Behold, a people has come out of Egypt, and it covers the face of the earth. Now come, curse them for me. Perhaps I shall be able to fight against them and drive them out.’”
verses.kjv: Behold, there is a people come out of Egypt, which covereth the face of the earth: come now, curse me them; peradventure I shall be able to overcome them, and drive them out.
verses.bsb: ‘Behold, a people has come out of Egypt, and they cover the face of the land. Now come and put a curse on them for me. Perhaps I may be able to fight against them and drive them away.’”

verses.esv: God said to Balaam, “You shall not go with them. You shall not curse the people, for they are blessed.”
verses.kjv: And God said unto Balaam, Thou shalt not go with them; thou shalt not curse the people: for they are blessed.
verses.bsb: But God said to Balaam, “Do not go with them. You are not to curse this people, for they are blessed.”

verses.esv: So Balaam rose in the morning and said to the princes of Balak, “Go to your own land, for the LORD has refused to let me go with you.”
verses.kjv: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and said unto the princes of Balak, Get you into your land: for the Lord refuseth to give me leave to go with you.
verses.bsb: So Balaam got up the next morning and said to Balak’s princes, “Go back to your homeland, because the LORD has refused to let me go with you.”

verses.esv: So the princes of Moab rose and went to Balak and said, “Balaam refuses to come with us.”
verses.kjv: And the princes of Moab rose up, and they went unto Balak, and said, Balaam refuseth to come with us.
verses.bsb: And the princes of Moab arose, returned to Balak, and said, “Balaam refused to come with us.”

verses.esv: Once again Balak sent princes, more in number and more honorable than these.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Balak sent yet again princes, more, and more honourable than they.
verses.bsb: Then Balak sent other princes, more numerous and more distinguished than the first messengers.

verses.esv: And they came to Balaam and said to him, “Thus says Balak the son of Zippor: ‘Let nothing hinder you from coming to me,
verses.kjv: And they came to Balaam, and said to him, Thus saith Balak the son of Zippor, Let nothing, I pray thee, hinder thee from coming unto me:
verses.bsb: They came to Balaam and said, “This is what Balak son of Zippor says: ‘Please let nothing hinder you from coming to me,

verses.esv: for I will surely do you great honor, and whatever you say to me I will do. Come, curse this people for me.’”
verses.kjv: For I will promote thee unto very great honour, and I will do whatsoever thou sayest unto me: come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.
verses.bsb: for I will honor you richly and do whatever you say. So please come and put a curse on this people for me!’”

verses.esv: But Balaam answered and said to the servants of Balak, “Though Balak were to give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not go beyond the command of the LORD my God to do less or more.
verses.kjv: And Balaam answered and said unto the servants of Balak, If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the word of the Lord my God, to do less or more.
verses.bsb: But Balaam replied to the servants of Balak, “If Balak were to give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not do anything small or great to go beyond the command of the LORD my God.

verses.esv: So you, too, please stay here tonight, that I may know what more the LORD will say to me.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore, I pray you, tarry ye also here this night, that I may know what the Lord will say unto me more.
verses.bsb: So now, please stay here overnight as the others did, that I may find out what else the LORD has to tell me.”

verses.esv: And God came to Balaam at night and said to him, “If the men have come to call you, rise, go with them; but only do what I tell you.”
verses.kjv: And God came unto Balaam at night, and said unto him, If the men come to call thee, rise up, and go with them; but yet the word which I shall say unto thee, that shalt thou do.
verses.bsb: That night God came to Balaam and said, “Since these men have come to summon you, get up and go with them, but you must only do what I tell you.”

verses.esv: So Balaam rose in the morning and saddled his donkey and went with the princes of Moab.
verses.kjv: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and saddled his ass, and went with the princes of Moab.
verses.bsb: So in the morning Balaam got up, saddled his donkey, and went with the princes of Moab.

verses.esv: But God's anger was kindled because he went, and the angel of the LORD took his stand in the way as his adversary. Now he was riding on the donkey, and his two servants were with him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God’s anger was kindled because he went: and the angel of the Lord stood in the way for an adversary against him. Now he was riding upon his ass, and his two servants were with him.
verses.bsb: Then God’s anger was kindled because Balaam was going along, and the angel of the LORD stood in the road to oppose him. Balaam was riding his donkey, and his two servants were with him.

verses.esv: And the donkey saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road, with a drawn sword in his hand. And the donkey turned aside out of the road and went into the field. And Balaam struck the donkey, to turn her into the road.
verses.kjv: And the ass saw the angel of the Lord standing in the way, and his sword drawn in his hand: and the ass turned aside out of the way, and went into the field: and Balaam smote the ass, to turn her into the way.
verses.bsb: When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road with a drawn sword in his hand, she turned off the path and went into a field. So Balaam beat her to return her to the path.

verses.esv: Then the angel of the LORD stood in a narrow path between the vineyards, with a wall on either side.
verses.kjv: But the angel of the Lord stood in a path of the vineyards, a wall being on this side, and a wall on that side.
verses.bsb: Then the angel of the LORD stood in a narrow passage between two vineyards, with walls on either side.

verses.esv: And when the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she pushed against the wall and pressed Balaam's foot against the wall. So he struck her again.
verses.kjv: And when the ass saw the angel of the Lord, she thrust herself unto the wall, and crushed Balaam’s foot against the wall: and he smote her again.
verses.bsb: And the donkey saw the angel of the LORD and pressed herself against the wall, crushing Balaam’s foot against it. So he beat her once again.

verses.esv: Then the angel of the LORD went ahead and stood in a narrow place, where there was no way to turn either to the right or to the left.
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord went further, and stood in a narrow place, where was no way to turn either to the right hand or to the left.
verses.bsb: And the angel of the LORD moved on ahead and stood in a narrow place where there was no room to turn to the right or left.

verses.esv: When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she lay down under Balaam. And Balaam's anger was kindled, and he struck the donkey with his staff.
verses.kjv: And when the ass saw the angel of the Lord, she fell down under Balaam: and Balaam’s anger was kindled, and he smote the ass with a staff.
verses.bsb: When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she lay down under Balaam, and he became furious and beat her with his staff.

verses.esv: Then the LORD opened the mouth of the donkey, and she said to Balaam, “What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?”
verses.kjv: And the Lord opened the mouth of the ass, and she said unto Balaam, What have I done unto thee, that thou hast smitten me these three times?
verses.bsb: Then the LORD opened the donkey’s mouth, and she said to Balaam, “What have I done to you that you have beaten me these three times?”

verses.esv: And Balaam said to the donkey, “Because you have made a fool of me. I wish I had a sword in my hand, for then I would kill you.”
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto the ass, Because thou hast mocked me: I would there were a sword in mine hand, for now would I kill thee.
verses.bsb: Balaam answered the donkey, “You have made a fool of me! If I had a sword in my hand, I would kill you right now!”

verses.esv: And the donkey said to Balaam, “Am I not your donkey, on which you have ridden all your life long to this day? Is it my habit to treat you this way?” And he said, “No.”
verses.kjv: And the ass said unto Balaam, Am not I thine ass, upon which thou hast ridden ever since I was thine unto this day? was I ever wont to do so unto thee? And he said, Nay.
verses.bsb: But the donkey said to Balaam, “Am I not the donkey you have ridden all your life until today? Have I ever treated you this way before?”  “No,” he replied.

verses.esv: Then the LORD opened the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel of the LORD standing in the way, with his drawn sword in his hand. And he bowed down and fell on his face.
verses.kjv: Then the Lord opened the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel of the Lord standing in the way, and his sword drawn in his hand: and he bowed down his head, and fell flat on his face.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD opened Balaam’s eyes, and he saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road with a drawn sword in his hand. And Balaam bowed low and fell facedown.

verses.esv: And the angel of the LORD said to him, “Why have you struck your donkey these three times? Behold, I have come out to oppose you because your way is perverse before me.
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord said unto him, Wherefore hast thou smitten thine ass these three times? behold, I went out to withstand thee, because thy way is perverse before me:
verses.bsb: The angel of the LORD asked him, “Why have you beaten your donkey these three times? Behold, I have come out to oppose you, because your way is perverse before me.

verses.esv: The donkey saw me and turned aside before me these three times. If she had not turned aside from me, surely just now I would have killed you and let her live.”
verses.kjv: And the ass saw me, and turned from me these three times: unless she had turned from me, surely now also I had slain thee, and saved her alive.
verses.bsb: The donkey saw me and turned away from me these three times. If she had not turned away, then by now I would surely have killed you and let her live.”

verses.esv: Then Balaam said to the angel of the LORD, “I have sinned, for I did not know that you stood in the road against me. Now therefore, if it is evil in your sight, I will turn back.”
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto the angel of the Lord, I have sinned; for I knew not that thou stoodest in the way against me: now therefore, if it displease thee, I will get me back again.
verses.bsb: “I have sinned,” Balaam said to the angel of the LORD, “for I did not realize that you were standing in the road to confront me. And now, if this is displeasing in your sight, I will go back home.”

verses.esv: And the angel of the LORD said to Balaam, “Go with the men, but speak only the word that I tell you.” So Balaam went on with the princes of Balak.
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord said unto Balaam, Go with the men: but only the word that I shall speak unto thee, that thou shalt speak. So Balaam went with the princes of Balak.
verses.bsb: But the angel of the LORD said to Balaam, “Go with the men, but you are to speak only what I tell you.” So Balaam went with the princes of Balak.

verses.esv: When Balak heard that Balaam had come, he went out to meet him at the city of Moab, on the border formed by the Arnon, at the extremity of the border.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Balak heard that Balaam was come, he went out to meet him unto a city of Moab, which is in the border of Arnon, which is in the utmost coast.
verses.bsb: When Balak heard that Balaam was coming, he went out to meet him at the Moabite city on the Arnon border, at the edge of his territory.

verses.esv: And Balak said to Balaam, “Did I not send to you to call you? Why did you not come to me? Am I not able to honor you?”
verses.kjv: And Balak said unto Balaam, Did I not earnestly send unto thee to call thee? wherefore camest thou not unto me? am I not able indeed to promote thee to honour?
verses.bsb: And he said to Balaam, “Did I not send you an urgent summons? Why did you not come to me? Am I really not able to richly reward you?”

verses.esv: Balaam said to Balak, “Behold, I have come to you! Have I now any power of my own to speak anything? The word that God puts in my mouth, that must I speak.”
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto Balak, Lo, I am come unto thee: have I now any power at all to say any thing? the word that God putteth in my mouth, that shall I speak.
verses.bsb: “See, I have come to you,” Balaam replied, “but can I say just anything? I must speak only the word that God puts in my mouth.”

verses.esv: Then Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath-huzoth.
verses.kjv: And Balaam went with Balak, and they came unto Kirjath-huzoth.
verses.bsb: So Balaam accompanied Balak, and they came to Kiriath-huzoth.

verses.esv: And Balak sacrificed oxen and sheep, and sent for Balaam and for the princes who were with him.
verses.kjv: And Balak offered oxen and sheep, and sent to Balaam, and to the princes that were with him.
verses.bsb: Balak sacrificed cattle and sheep, and he gave portions to Balaam and the princes who were with him.

verses.esv: And in the morning Balak took Balaam and brought him up to Bamoth-baal, and from there he saw a fraction of the people.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Balak took Balaam, and brought him up into the high places of Baal, that thence he might see the utmost part of the people.
verses.bsb: The next morning, Balak took Balaam and brought him up to Bamoth-baal. From there he could see the outskirts of the camp of the people.

verses.esv: And Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me here seven altars, and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.”
verses.kjv: AND Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven oxen and seven rams.
verses.bsb: Then Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me seven altars here, and prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams.”

verses.esv: Balak did as Balaam had said. And Balak and Balaam offered on each altar a bull and a ram.
verses.kjv: And Balak did as Balaam had spoken; and Balak and Balaam offered on every altar a bullock and a ram.
verses.bsb: So Balak did as Balaam had instructed, and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

verses.esv: And Balaam said to Balak, “Stand beside your burnt offering, and I will go. Perhaps the LORD will come to meet me, and whatever he shows me I will tell you.” And he went to a bare height,
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto Balak, Stand by thy burnt offering, and I will go: peradventure the Lord will come to meet me: and whatsoever he sheweth me I will tell thee. And he went to an high place.
verses.bsb: “Stay here by your burnt offering while I am gone,” Balaam said to Balak. “Perhaps the LORD will meet with me. And whatever He reveals to me, I will tell you.”  So Balaam went off to a barren height,

verses.esv: and God met Balaam. And Balaam said to him, “I have arranged the seven altars and I have offered on each altar a bull and a ram.”
verses.kjv: And God met Balaam: and he said unto him, I have prepared seven altars, and I have offered upon every altar a bullock and a ram.
verses.bsb: and God met with him. “I have set up seven altars,” Balaam said, “and on each altar I have offered a bull and a ram.”

verses.esv: And the LORD put a word in Balaam's mouth and said, “Return to Balak, and thus you shall speak.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord put a word in Balaam’s mouth, and said, Return unto Balak, and thus thou shalt speak.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD put a message in Balaam’s mouth, saying, “Return to Balak and give him this message.”

verses.esv: And he returned to him, and behold, he and all the princes of Moab were standing beside his burnt offering.
verses.kjv: And he returned unto him, and, lo, he stood by his burnt sacrifice, he, and all the princes of Moab.
verses.bsb: So he returned to Balak, who was standing there beside his burnt offering, with all the princes of Moab.

verses.esv: And Balaam took up his discourse and said, “From Aram Balak has brought me, the king of Moab from the eastern mountains: ‘Come, curse Jacob for me, and come, denounce Israel!’
verses.kjv: And he took up his parable, and said, Balak the king of Moab hath brought me from Aram, out of the mountains of the east, saying, Come, curse me Jacob, and come, defy Israel.
verses.bsb: And Balaam lifted up an oracle, saying:  “Balak brought me from Aram, the king of Moab from the mountains of the east. ‘Come,’ he said, ‘put a curse on Jacob for me; come and denounce Israel!’

verses.esv: How can I curse whom God has not cursed? How can I denounce whom the LORD has not denounced?
verses.kjv: How shall I curse, whom God hath not cursed? or how shall I defy, whom the Lord hath not defied?
verses.bsb: How can I curse what God has not cursed? How can I denounce what the LORD has not denounced?

verses.esv: For from the top of the crags I see him, from the hills I behold him; behold, a people dwelling alone, and not counting itself among the nations!
verses.kjv: For from the top of the rocks I see him, and from the hills I behold him: lo, the people shall dwell alone, and shall not be reckoned among the nations.
verses.bsb: For I see them from atop the rocky cliffs, and I watch them from the hills. Behold, a people dwelling apart, not reckoning themselves among the nations.

verses.esv: Who can count the dust of Jacob or number the fourth part of Israel? Let me die the death of the upright, and let my end be like his!”
verses.kjv: Who can count the dust of Jacob, and the number of the fourth part of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous, and let my last end be like his!
verses.bsb: Who can count the dust of Jacob or number even a fourth of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous; let my end be like theirs!”

verses.esv: And Balak said to Balaam, “What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, and behold, you have done nothing but bless them.”
verses.kjv: And Balak said unto Balaam, What hast thou done unto me? I took thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast blessed them altogether.
verses.bsb: Then Balak said to Balaam, “What have you done to me? I brought you here to curse my enemies, and behold, you have only blessed them!”

verses.esv: And he answered and said, “Must I not take care to speak what the LORD puts in my mouth?”
verses.kjv: And he answered and said, Must I not take heed to speak that which the Lord hath put in my mouth?
verses.bsb: But Balaam replied, “Should I not speak exactly what the LORD puts in my mouth?”

verses.esv: And Balak said to him, “Please come with me to another place, from which you may see them. You shall see only a fraction of them and shall not see them all. Then curse them for me from there.”
verses.kjv: And Balak said unto him, Come, I pray thee, with me unto another place, from whence thou mayest see them: thou shalt see but the utmost part of them, and shalt not see them all: and curse me them from thence.
verses.bsb: Then Balak said to him, “Please come with me to another place where you can see them. You will only see the outskirts of their camp—not all of them. And from there, curse them for me.”

verses.esv: And he took him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he brought him into the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar.
verses.bsb: So Balak took him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, where he built seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

verses.esv: Balaam said to Balak, “Stand here beside your burnt offering, while I meet the LORD over there.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto Balak, Stand here by thy burnt offering, while I meet the Lord yonder.
verses.bsb: Balaam said to Balak, “Stay here beside your burnt offering while I meet the LORD over there.”

verses.esv: And the LORD met Balaam and put a word in his mouth and said, “Return to Balak, and thus shall you speak.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said, Go again unto Balak, and say thus.
verses.bsb: And the LORD met with Balaam and put a message in his mouth, saying, “Return to Balak and speak what I tell you.”

verses.esv: And he came to him, and behold, he was standing beside his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him. And Balak said to him, “What has the LORD spoken?”
verses.kjv: And when he came to him, behold, he stood by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him. And Balak said unto him, What hath the Lord spoken?
verses.bsb: So he returned to Balak, who was standing there by his burnt offering with the princes of Moab.  “What did the LORD say?” Balak asked.

verses.esv: And Balaam took up his discourse and said, “Rise, Balak, and hear; give ear to me, O son of Zippor:
verses.kjv: And he took up his parable, and said, Rise up, Balak, and hear; hearken unto me, thou son of Zippor:
verses.bsb: Then Balaam lifted up an oracle, saying:  “Arise, O Balak, and listen; give ear to me, O son of Zippor.

verses.esv: God is not man, that he should lie, or a son of man, that he should change his mind. Has he said, and will he not do it? Or has he spoken, and will he not fulfill it?
verses.kjv: God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent: hath he said, and shall he not do it? or hath he spoken, and shall he not make it good?
verses.bsb: God is not a man, that He should lie, or a son of man, that He should change His mind. Does He speak and not act? Does He promise and not fulfill?

verses.esv: Behold, I received a command to bless: he has blessed, and I cannot revoke it.
verses.kjv: Behold, I have received commandment to bless: and he hath blessed; and I cannot reverse it.
verses.bsb: I have indeed received a command to bless; He has blessed, and I cannot change it.

verses.esv: He has not beheld misfortune in Jacob, nor has he seen trouble in Israel. The LORD their God is with them, and the shout of a king is among them.
verses.kjv: He hath not beheld iniquity in Jacob, neither hath he seen perverseness in Israel: the Lord his God is with him, and the shout of a king is among them.
verses.bsb: He considers no disaster for Jacob; He sees no trouble for Israel. The LORD their God is with them, and the shout of the King is among them.

verses.esv: God brings them out of Egypt and is for them like the horns of the wild ox.
verses.kjv: God brought them out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn.
verses.bsb: God brought them out of Egypt with strength like a wild ox.

verses.esv: For there is no enchantment against Jacob, no divination against Israel; now it shall be said of Jacob and Israel, ‘What has God wrought!’
verses.kjv: Surely there is no enchantment against Jacob, neither is there any divination against Israel: according to this time it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, What hath God wrought!
verses.bsb: For there is no spell against Jacob and no divination against Israel. It will now be said of Jacob and Israel, ‘What great things God has done!’

verses.esv: Behold, a people! As a lioness it rises up and as a lion it lifts itself; it does not lie down until it has devoured the prey and drunk the blood of the slain.”
verses.kjv: Behold, the people shall rise up as a great lion, and lift up himself as a young lion: he shall not lie down until he eat of the prey, and drink the blood of the slain.
verses.bsb: Behold, the people rise like a lioness; they rouse themselves like a lion, not resting until they devour their prey and drink the blood of the slain.”

verses.esv: And Balak said to Balaam, “Do not curse them at all, and do not bless them at all.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Balak said unto Balaam, Neither curse them at all, nor bless them at all.
verses.bsb: Now Balak said to Balaam, “Then neither curse them at all nor bless them at all!”

verses.esv: But Balaam answered Balak, “Did I not tell you, ‘All that the LORD says, that I must do’?”
verses.kjv: But Balaam answered and said unto Balak, Told not I thee, saying, All that the Lord speaketh, that I must do?
verses.bsb: But Balaam replied, “Did I not tell you that whatever the LORD says, I must do?”

verses.esv: And Balak said to Balaam, “Come now, I will take you to another place. Perhaps it will please God that you may curse them for me from there.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Balak said unto Balaam, Come, I pray thee, I will bring thee unto another place; peradventure it will please God that thou mayest curse me them from thence.
verses.bsb: “Please come,” said Balak, “I will take you to another place. Perhaps it will please God that you curse them for me from there.”

verses.esv: So Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which overlooks the desert.
verses.kjv: And Balak brought Balaam unto the top of Peor, that looketh toward Jeshimon.
verses.bsb: And Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which overlooks the wasteland.

verses.esv: And Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me here seven altars and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.”
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven bullocks and seven rams.
verses.bsb: Then Balaam said, “Build for me seven altars here, and prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams.”

verses.esv: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
verses.kjv: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar.
verses.bsb: So Balak did as Balaam had instructed, and he offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

verses.esv: When Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel, he did not go, as at other times, to look for omens, but set his face toward the wilderness.
verses.kjv: AND when Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord to bless Israel, he went not, as at other times, to seek for enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.
verses.bsb: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel, he did not resort to sorcery as on previous occasions, but he turned his face toward the wilderness.

verses.esv: And Balaam lifted up his eyes and saw Israel camping tribe by tribe. And the Spirit of God came upon him,
verses.kjv: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents according to their tribes; and the spirit of God came upon him.
verses.bsb: When Balaam looked up and saw Israel encamped tribe by tribe, the Spirit of God came upon him,

verses.esv: and he took up his discourse and said, “The oracle of Balaam the son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is opened,
verses.kjv: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
verses.bsb: and he lifted up an oracle, saying:  “This is the prophecy of Balaam son of Beor, the prophecy of a man whose eyes are open,

verses.esv: the oracle of him who hears the words of God, who sees the vision of the Almighty, falling down with his eyes uncovered:
verses.kjv: He hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
verses.bsb: the prophecy of one who hears the words of God, who sees a vision from the Almighty, who bows down with eyes wide open:

verses.esv: How lovely are your tents, O Jacob, your encampments, O Israel!
verses.kjv: How goodly are thy tents, O Jacob, and thy tabernacles, O Israel!
verses.bsb: How lovely are your tents, O Jacob, your dwellings, O Israel!

verses.esv: Like palm groves that stretch afar, like gardens beside a river, like aloes that the LORD has planted, like cedar trees beside the waters.
verses.kjv: As the valleys are they spread forth, as gardens by the river’s side, as the trees of lign aloes which the Lord hath planted, and as cedar trees beside the waters.
verses.bsb: They spread out like palm groves, like gardens beside a stream, like aloes the LORD has planted, like cedars beside the waters.

verses.esv: Water shall flow from his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters; his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.
verses.kjv: He shall pour the water out of his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters, and his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: Water will flow from his buckets, and his seed will have abundant water. His king will be greater than Agag, and his kingdom will be exalted.

verses.esv: God brings him out of Egypt and is for him like the horns of the wild ox; he shall eat up the nations, his adversaries, and shall break their bones in pieces and pierce them through with his arrows.
verses.kjv: God brought him forth out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn: he shall eat up the nations his enemies, and shall break their bones, and pierce them through with his arrows.
verses.bsb: God brought him out of Egypt with strength like a wild ox, to devour hostile nations and crush their bones, to pierce them with arrows.

verses.esv: He crouched, he lay down like a lion and like a lioness; who will rouse him up? Blessed are those who bless you, and cursed are those who curse you.”
verses.kjv: He couched, he lay down as a lion, and as a great lion: who shall stir him up? Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.
verses.bsb: He crouches, he lies down like a lion; like a lioness, who dares to rouse him? Blessed are those who bless you and cursed are those who curse you.”

verses.esv: And Balak's anger was kindled against Balaam, and he struck his hands together. And Balak said to Balaam, “I called you to curse my enemies, and behold, you have blessed them these three times.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Balak’s anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together: and Balak said unto Balaam, I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them these three times.
verses.bsb: Then Balak’s anger burned against Balaam, and he struck his hands together and said to Balaam, “I summoned you to curse my enemies, but behold, you have persisted in blessing them these three times.

verses.esv: Therefore now flee to your own place. I said, ‘I will certainly honor you,’ but the LORD has held you back from honor.”
verses.kjv: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honour; but, lo, the Lord hath kept thee back from honour.
verses.bsb: Therefore, flee at once to your home! I said I would richly reward you, but instead the LORD has denied your reward.”

verses.esv: And Balaam said to Balak, “Did I not tell your messengers whom you sent to me,
verses.kjv: And Balaam said unto Balak, Spake I not also to thy messengers which thou sentest unto me, saying,
verses.bsb: Balaam answered Balak, “Did I not already tell the messengers you sent me

verses.esv: ‘If Balak should give me his house full of silver and gold, I would not be able to go beyond the word of the LORD, to do either good or bad of my own will. What the LORD speaks, that will I speak’?
verses.kjv: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the commandment of the Lord, to do either good or bad of mine own mind; but what the Lord saith, that will I speak?
verses.bsb: that even if Balak were to give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not do anything of my own accord, good or bad, to go beyond the command of the LORD? I will speak whatever the LORD says.

verses.esv: And now, behold, I am going to my people. Come, I will let you know what this people will do to your people in the latter days.”
verses.kjv: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come therefore, and I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.
verses.bsb: Now I am going back to my people, but come, let me warn you what this people will do to your people in the days to come.”

verses.esv: And he took up his discourse and said, “The oracle of Balaam the son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is opened,
verses.kjv: ¶ And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
verses.bsb: Then Balaam lifted up an oracle, saying,  “This is the prophecy of Balaam son of Beor, the prophecy of a man whose eyes are open,

verses.esv: the oracle of him who hears the words of God, and knows the knowledge of the Most High, who sees the vision of the Almighty, falling down with his eyes uncovered:
verses.kjv: He hath said, which heard the words of God, and knew the knowledge of the most High, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
verses.bsb: the prophecy of one who hears the words of God, who has knowledge from the Most High, who sees a vision from the Almighty, who bows down with eyes wide open:

verses.esv: I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near: a star shall come out of Jacob, and a scepter shall rise out of Israel; it shall crush the forehead of Moab and break down all the sons of Sheth.
verses.kjv: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him, but not nigh: there shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre shall rise out of Israel, and shall smite the corners of Moab, and destroy all the children of Sheth.
verses.bsb: I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near. A star will come forth from Jacob, and a scepter will arise from Israel. He will crush the skulls of Moab and strike down all the sons of Sheth.

verses.esv: Edom shall be dispossessed; Seir also, his enemies, shall be dispossessed. Israel is doing valiantly.
verses.kjv: And Edom shall be a possession, Seir also shall be a possession for his enemies; and Israel shall do valiantly.
verses.bsb: Edom will become a possession, as will Seir, his enemy; but Israel will perform with valor.

verses.esv: And one from Jacob shall exercise dominion and destroy the survivors of cities!”
verses.kjv: Out of Jacob shall come he that shall have dominion, and shall destroy him that remaineth of the city.
verses.bsb: A ruler will come from Jacob and destroy the survivors of the city.”

verses.esv: Then he looked on Amalek and took up his discourse and said, “Amalek was the first among the nations, but its end is utter destruction.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he looked on Amalek, he took up his parable, and said, Amalek was the first of the nations; but his latter end shall be that he perish for ever.
verses.bsb: Then Balaam saw Amalek and lifted up an oracle, saying:  “Amalek was first among the nations, but his end is destruction.”

verses.esv: And he looked on the Kenite, and took up his discourse and said, “Enduring is your dwelling place, and your nest is set in the rock.
verses.kjv: And he looked on the Kenites, and took up his parable, and said, Strong is thy dwellingplace, and thou puttest thy nest in a rock.
verses.bsb: Next he saw the Kenites and lifted up an oracle, saying:  “Your dwelling place is secure, and your nest is set in a cliff.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, Kain shall be burned when Asshur takes you away captive.”
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the Kenite shall be wasted, until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.
verses.bsb: Yet Kain will be destroyed when Asshur takes you captive.”

verses.esv: And he took up his discourse and said, “Alas, who shall live when God does this?
verses.kjv: And he took up his parable, and said, Alas, who shall live when God doeth this!
verses.bsb: Once more Balaam lifted up an oracle, saying:  “Ah, who can live unless God has ordained it?

verses.esv: But ships shall come from Kittim and shall afflict Asshur and Eber; and he too shall come to utter destruction.”
verses.kjv: And ships shall come from the coast of Chittim, and shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber, and he also shall perish for ever.
verses.bsb: Ships will come from the coasts of Cyprus; they will subdue Asshur and Eber, but they too will perish forever.”

verses.esv: Then Balaam rose and went back to his place. And Balak also went his way.
verses.kjv: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place: and Balak also went his way.
verses.bsb: Then Balaam arose and returned to his homeland, and Balak also went on his way.

verses.esv: While Israel lived in Shittim, the people began to whore with the daughters of Moab.
verses.kjv: AND Israel abode in Shittim, and the people began to commit whoredom with the daughters of Moab.
verses.bsb: While Israel was staying in Shittim, the men began to indulge in sexual immorality with the daughters of Moab,

verses.esv: These invited the people to the sacrifices of their gods, and the people ate and bowed down to their gods.
verses.kjv: And they called the people unto the sacrifices of their gods: and the people did eat, and bowed down to their gods.
verses.bsb: who also invited them to the sacrifices for their gods. And the people ate and bowed down to these gods.

verses.esv: So Israel yoked himself to Baal of Peor. And the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel.
verses.kjv: And Israel joined himself unto Baal-peor: and the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel.
verses.bsb: So Israel joined in worshiping Baal of Peor, and the anger of the LORD burned against them.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “Take all the chiefs of the people and hang them in the sun before the LORD, that the fierce anger of the LORD may turn away from Israel.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Take all the heads of the people, and hang them up before the Lord against the sun, that the fierce anger of the Lord may be turned away from Israel.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Take all the leaders of the people and execute them in broad daylight before the LORD, so that His fierce anger may turn away from Israel.”

verses.esv: And Moses said to the judges of Israel, “Each of you kill those of his men who have yoked themselves to Baal of Peor.”
verses.kjv: And Moses said unto the judges of Israel, Slay ye every one his men that were joined unto Baal-peor.
verses.bsb: So Moses told the judges of Israel, “Each of you must kill all of his men who have joined in worshiping Baal of Peor.”

verses.esv: And behold, one of the people of Israel came and brought a Midianite woman to his family, in the sight of Moses and in the sight of the whole congregation of the people of Israel, while they were weeping in the entrance of the tent of meeting.
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, one of the children of Israel came and brought unto his brethren a Midianitish woman in the sight of Moses, and in the sight of all the congregation of the children of Israel, who were weeping before the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
verses.bsb: Just then an Israelite man brought to his family a Midianite woman in the sight of Moses and the whole congregation of Israel while they were weeping at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

verses.esv: When Phinehas the son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, saw it, he rose and left the congregation and took a spear in his hand
verses.kjv: And when Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, saw it, he rose up from among the congregation, and took a javelin in his hand;
verses.bsb: On seeing this, Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, got up from the assembly, took a spear in his hand,

verses.esv: and went after the man of Israel into the chamber and pierced both of them, the man of Israel and the woman through her belly. Thus the plague on the people of Israel was stopped.
verses.kjv: And he went after the man of Israel into the tent, and thrust both of them through, the man of Israel, and the woman through her belly. So the plague was stayed from the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: followed the Israelite into his tent, and drove the spear through both of them—through the Israelite and on through the belly of the woman.  So the plague against the Israelites was halted,

verses.esv: Nevertheless, those who died by the plague were twenty-four thousand.
verses.kjv: And those that died in the plague were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: but those who died in the plague numbered 24,000.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Phinehas the son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, has turned back my wrath from the people of Israel, in that he was jealous with my jealousy among them, so that I did not consume the people of Israel in my jealousy.
verses.kjv: Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, hath turned my wrath away from the children of Israel, while he was zealous for my sake among them, that I consumed not the children of Israel in my jealousy.
verses.bsb: “Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, has turned My wrath away from the Israelites; for he was zealous for My sake among them, so that I did not consume the Israelites in My zeal.

verses.esv: Therefore say, ‘Behold, I give to him my covenant of peace,
verses.kjv: Wherefore say, Behold, I give unto him my covenant of peace:
verses.bsb: Declare, therefore, that I am granting him My covenant of peace.

verses.esv: and it shall be to him and to his descendants after him the covenant of a perpetual priesthood, because he was jealous for his God and made atonement for the people of Israel.’”
verses.kjv: And he shall have it, and his seed after him, even the covenant of an everlasting priesthood; because he was zealous for his God, and made an atonement for the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: It will be a covenant of permanent priesthood for him and his descendants, because he was zealous for his God and made atonement for the Israelites.”

verses.esv: The name of the slain man of Israel, who was killed with the Midianite woman, was Zimri the son of Salu, chief of a father's house belonging to the Simeonites.
verses.kjv: Now the name of the Israelite that was slain, even that was slain with the Midianitish woman, was Zimri, the son of Salu, a prince of a chief house among the Simeonites.
verses.bsb: The name of the Israelite who was slain with the Midianite woman was Zimri son of Salu, the leader of a Simeonite family.

verses.esv: And the name of the Midianite woman who was killed was Cozbi the daughter of Zur, who was the tribal head of a father's house in Midian.
verses.kjv: And the name of the Midianitish woman that was slain was Cozbi, the daughter of Zur; he was head over a people, and of a chief house in Midian.
verses.bsb: And the name of the slain Midianite woman was Cozbi, the daughter of Zur, a tribal chief of a Midianite family.

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Harass the Midianites and strike them down,
verses.kjv: Vex the Midianites, and smite them:
verses.bsb: “Attack the Midianites and strike them dead.

verses.esv: for they have harassed you with their wiles, with which they beguiled you in the matter of Peor, and in the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of the chief of Midian, their sister, who was killed on the day of the plague on account of Peor.”
verses.kjv: For they vex you with their wiles, wherewith they have beguiled you in the matter of Peor, and in the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of a prince of Midian, their sister, which was slain in the day of the plague for Peor’s sake.
verses.bsb: For they assailed you deceitfully when they seduced you in the matter of Peor and their sister Cozbi, the daughter of the Midianite leader, the woman who was killed on the day the plague came because of Peor.”

verses.esv: After the plague, the LORD said to Moses and to Eleazar the son of Aaron, the priest,
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after the plague, that the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying,
verses.bsb: After the plague had ended, the LORD said to Moses and Eleazar son of Aaron the priest,

verses.esv: “Take a census of all the congregation of the people of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, by their fathers' houses, all in Israel who are able to go to war.”
verses.kjv: Take the sum of all the congregation of the children of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, throughout their fathers’ house, all that are able to go to war in Israel.
verses.bsb: “Take a census of the whole congregation of Israel by the houses of their fathers—all those twenty years of age or older who can serve in the army of Israel.”

verses.esv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,
verses.kjv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest spake with them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
verses.bsb: So on the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho, Moses and Eleazar the priest issued the instruction,

verses.esv: “Take a census of the people, from twenty years old and upward,” as the LORD commanded Moses. The people of Israel who came out of the land of Egypt were:
verses.kjv: Take the sum of the people, from twenty years old and upward; as the Lord commanded Moses and the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: “Take a census of the men twenty years of age or older, as the LORD has commanded Moses.”  And these were the Israelites who came out of the land of Egypt:

verses.esv: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel; the sons of Reuben: of Hanoch, the clan of the Hanochites; of Pallu, the clan of the Palluites;
verses.kjv: ¶ Reuben, the eldest son of Israel: the children of Reuben; Hanoch, of whom cometh the family of the Hanochites: of Pallu, the family of the Palluites:
verses.bsb: Reuben was the firstborn of Israel. These were the descendants of Reuben:  The Hanochite clan from Hanoch,   the Palluite clan from Pallu,

verses.esv: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Carmi, the clan of the Carmites.
verses.kjv: Of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Carmi, the family of the Carmites.
verses.bsb: the Hezronite clan from Hezron,   and the Carmite clan from Carmi.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the Reubenites, and those listed were 43,730.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the Reubenites: and they that were numbered of them were forty and three thousand and seven hundred and thirty.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Reuben, and their registration numbered 43,730.

verses.esv: And the sons of Pallu: Eliab.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Pallu; Eliab.
verses.bsb: Now the son of Pallu was Eliab,

verses.esv: The sons of Eliab: Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. These are the Dathan and Abiram, chosen from the congregation, who contended against Moses and Aaron in the company of Korah, when they contended against the LORD
verses.kjv: And the sons of Eliab; Nemuel, and Dathan, and Abiram. This is that Dathan and Abiram, which were famous in the congregation, who strove against Moses and against Aaron in the company of Korah, when they strove against the Lord:
verses.bsb: and the sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram.  It was Dathan and Abiram, chosen by the congregation, who fought against Moses and Aaron with the followers of Korah who rebelled against the LORD.

verses.esv: and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up together with Korah, when that company died, when the fire devoured 250 men, and they became a warning.
verses.kjv: And the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up together with Korah, when that company died, what time the fire devoured two hundred and fifty men: and they became a sign.
verses.bsb: And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them along with Korah, whose followers died when the fire consumed 250 men. They serve as a warning sign.

verses.esv: But the sons of Korah did not die.
verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the children of Korah died not.
verses.bsb: However, the line of Korah did not die out.

verses.esv: The sons of Simeon according to their clans: of Nemuel, the clan of the Nemuelites; of Jamin, the clan of the Jaminites; of Jachin, the clan of the Jachinites;
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Simeon after their families: of Nemuel, the family of the Nemuelites: of Jamin, the family of the Jaminites: of Jachin, the family of the Jachinites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Simeon by their clans:  The Nemuelite clan from Nemuel,   the Jaminite clan from Jamin,   the Jachinite clan from Jachin,

verses.esv: of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites; of Shaul, the clan of the Shaulites.
verses.kjv: Of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites: of Shaul, the family of the Shaulites.
verses.bsb: the Zerahite clan from Zerah,   and the Shaulite clan from Shaul.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the Simeonites, 22,200.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the Simeonites, twenty and two thousand and two hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Simeon, and there were 22,200 men.

verses.esv: The sons of Gad according to their clans: of Zephon, the clan of the Zephonites; of Haggi, the clan of the Haggites; of Shuni, the clan of the Shunites;
verses.kjv: ¶ The children of Gad after their families: of Zephon, the family of the Zephonites: of Haggi, the family of the Haggites: of Shuni, the family of the Shunites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Gad by their clans:  The Zephonite clan from Zephon,   the Haggite clan from Haggi,   the Shunite clan from Shuni,

verses.esv: of Ozni, the clan of the Oznites; of Eri, the clan of the Erites;
verses.kjv: Of Ozni, the family of the Oznites: of Eri, the family of the Erites:
verses.bsb: the Oznite clan from Ozni,   the Erite clan from Eri,

verses.esv: of Arod, the clan of the Arodites; of Areli, the clan of the Arelites.
verses.kjv: Of Arod, the family of the Arodites: of Areli, the family of the Arelites.
verses.bsb: the Arodite clan from Arod,   and the Arelite clan from Areli.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the sons of Gad as they were listed, 40,500.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the children of Gad according to those that were numbered of them, forty thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Gad, and their registration numbered 40,500.

verses.esv: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan; and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Judah were Er and Onan: and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan, but they died in the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: And the sons of Judah according to their clans were: of Shelah, the clan of the Shelanites; of Perez, the clan of the Perezites; of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Judah after their families were; of Shelah, the family of the Shelanites: of Pharez, the family of the Pharzites: of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Judah by their clans:  The Shelanite clan from Shelah,   the Perezite clan from Perez,   and the Zerahite clan from Zerah.

verses.esv: And the sons of Perez were: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Hamul, the clan of the Hamulites.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Pharez were; of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Hamul, the family of the Hamulites.
verses.bsb: And these were the descendants of Perez:   the Hezronite clan from Hezron   and the Hamulite clan from Hamul.

verses.esv: These are the clans of Judah as they were listed, 76,500.
verses.kjv: These are the families of Judah according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and sixteen thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Judah, and their registration numbered 76,500.

verses.esv: The sons of Issachar according to their clans: of Tola, the clan of the Tolaites; of Puvah, the clan of the Punites;
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Issachar after their families: of Tola, the family of the Tolaites: of Pua, the family of the Punites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Issachar by their clans:  The Tolaite clan from Tola,   the Punite clan from Puvah,

verses.esv: of Jashub, the clan of the Jashubites; of Shimron, the clan of the Shimronites.
verses.kjv: Of Jashub, the family of the Jashubites: of Shimron, the family of the Shimronites.
verses.bsb: the Jashubite clan from Jashub,   and the Shimronite clan from Shimron.

verses.esv: These are the clans of Issachar as they were listed, 64,300.
verses.kjv: These are the families of Issachar according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and four thousand and three hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Issachar, and their registration numbered 64,300.

verses.esv: The sons of Zebulun, according to their clans: of Sered, the clan of the Seredites; of Elon, the clan of the Elonites; of Jahleel, the clan of the Jahleelites.
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Zebulun after their families: of Sered, the family of the Sardites: of Elon, the family of the Elonites: of Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Zebulun by their clans:  The Seredite clan from Sered,   the Elonite clan from Elon,   and the Jahleelite clan from Jahleel.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the Zebulunites as they were listed, 60,500.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the Zebulunites according to those that were numbered of them, threescore thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Zebulun, and their registration numbered 60,500.

verses.esv: The sons of Joseph according to their clans: Manasseh and Ephraim.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Joseph after their families were Manasseh and Ephraim.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Joseph included the clans of Manasseh and Ephraim.

verses.esv: The sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the clan of the Machirites; and Machir was the father of Gilead; of Gilead, the clan of the Gileadites.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the family of the Machirites: and Machir begat Gilead: of Gilead come the family of the Gileadites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Manasseh:  The Machirite clan from Machir, the father of Gilead,   and the Gileadite clan from Gilead.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Gilead: of Iezer, the clan of the Iezerites; of Helek, the clan of the Helekites;
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Gilead: of Jeezer, the family of the Jeezerites: of Helek, the family of the Helekites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Gilead:   the Iezerite clan from Iezer,   the Helekite clan from Helek,

verses.esv: and of Asriel, the clan of the Asrielites; and of Shechem, the clan of the Shechemites;
verses.kjv: And of Asriel, the family of the Asrielites: and of Shechem, the family of the Shechemites:
verses.bsb: the Asrielite clan from Asriel,   the Shechemite clan from Shechem,

verses.esv: and of Shemida, the clan of the Shemidaites; and of Hepher, the clan of the Hepherites.
verses.kjv: And of Shemida, the family of the Shemidaites: and of Hepher, the family of the Hepherites.
verses.bsb: the Shemidaite clan from Shemida,   and the Hepherite clan from Hepher.

verses.esv: Now Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters. And the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters: and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.bsb: Now Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons but only daughters. The names of his daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

verses.esv: These are the clans of Manasseh, and those listed were 52,700.
verses.kjv: These are the families of Manasseh, and those that were numbered of them, fifty and two thousand and seven hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Manasseh, and their registration numbered 52,700.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Ephraim according to their clans: of Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahites; of Becher, the clan of the Becherites; of Tahan, the clan of the Tahanites.
verses.kjv: ¶ These are the sons of Ephraim after their families: of Shuthelah, the family of the Shuthalhites: of Becher, the family of the Bachrites: of Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Ephraim by their clans:  The Shuthelahite clan from Shuthelah,   the Becherite clan from Becher,   and the Tahanite clan from Tahan.

verses.esv: And these are the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the clan of the Eranites.
verses.kjv: And these are the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the family of the Eranites.
verses.bsb: And the descendants of Shuthelah were the Eranite clan from Eran.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the sons of Ephraim as they were listed, 32,500. These are the sons of Joseph according to their clans.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Ephraim according to those that were numbered of them, thirty and two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph after their families.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Ephraim, and their registration numbered 32,500.  These clans were the descendants of Joseph.

verses.esv: The sons of Benjamin according to their clans: of Bela, the clan of the Belaites; of Ashbel, the clan of the Ashbelites; of Ahiram, the clan of the Ahiramites;
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Benjamin after their families: of Bela, the family of the Belaites: of Ashbel, the family of the Ashbelites: of Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Benjamin by their clans:  The Belaite clan from Bela,   the Ashbelite clan from Ashbel,   the Ahiramite clan from Ahiram,

verses.esv: of Shephupham, the clan of the Shuphamites; of Hupham, the clan of the Huphamites.
verses.kjv: Of Shupham, the family of the Shuphamites: of Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.
verses.bsb: the Shuphamite clan from Shupham,   and the Huphamite clan from Hupham.

verses.esv: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the clan of the Ardites; of Naaman, the clan of the Naamites.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the family of the Ardites: and of Naaman, the family of the Naamites.
verses.bsb: And the descendants of Bela from Ard and Naaman were the Ardite clan from Ard and the Naamite clan from Naaman.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Benjamin according to their clans, and those listed were 45,600.
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Benjamin after their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and six hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Benjamin, and their registration numbered 45,600.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Dan according to their clans: of Shuham, the clan of the Shuhamites. These are the clans of Dan according to their clans.
verses.kjv: ¶ These are the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan after their families.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Dan by their clans:  The Shuhamite clan from Shuham.   These were the clans of Dan.

verses.esv: All the clans of the Shuhamites, as they were listed, were 64,400.
verses.kjv: All the families of the Shuhamites, according to those that were numbered of them, were threescore and four thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: All of them were Shuhamite clans, and their registration numbered 64,400.

verses.esv: The sons of Asher according to their clans: of Imnah, the clan of the Imnites; of Ishvi, the clan of the Ishvites; of Beriah, the clan of the Beriites.
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the children of Asher after their families: of Jimna, the family of the Jimnites: of Jesui, the family of the Jesuites: of Beriah, the family of the Beriites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Asher by their clans:  The Imnite clan from Imnah,   the Ishvite clan from Ishvi,   and the Beriite clan from Beriah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the clan of the Heberites; of Malchiel, the clan of the Malchielites.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the family of the Heberites: of Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites.
verses.bsb: And these were the descendants of Beriah:   the Heberite clan from Heber   and the Malchielite clan from Malchiel.

verses.esv: And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah.
verses.kjv: And the name of the daughter of Asher was Sarah.
verses.bsb: And the name of Asher’s daughter was Serah.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the sons of Asher as they were listed, 53,400.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Asher according to those that were numbered of them; who were fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Asher, and their registration numbered 53,400.

verses.esv: The sons of Naphtali according to their clans: of Jahzeel, the clan of the Jahzeelites; of Guni, the clan of the Gunites;
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Naphtali after their families: of Jahzeel, the family of the Jahzeelites: of Guni, the family of the Gunites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Naphtali by their clans:  The Jahzeelite clan from Jahzeel,   the Gunite clan from Guni,

verses.esv: of Jezer, the clan of the Jezerites; of Shillem, the clan of the Shillemites.
verses.kjv: Of Jezer, the family of the Jezerites: of Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.
verses.bsb: the Jezerite clan from Jezer,   and the Shillemite clan from Shillem.

verses.esv: These are the clans of Naphtali according to their clans, and those listed were 45,400.
verses.kjv: These are the families of Naphtali according to their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Naphtali, and their registration numbered 45,400.

verses.esv: This was the list of the people of Israel, 601,730.
verses.kjv: These were the numbered of the children of Israel, six hundred thousand and a thousand seven hundred and thirty.
verses.bsb: These men of Israel numbered 601,730 in all.

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Among these the land shall be divided for inheritance according to the number of names.
verses.kjv: Unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names.
verses.bsb: “The land is to be divided among the tribes as an inheritance, according to the number of names.

verses.esv: To a large tribe you shall give a large inheritance, and to a small tribe you shall give a small inheritance; every tribe shall be given its inheritance in proportion to its list.
verses.kjv: To many thou shalt give the more inheritance, and to few thou shalt give the less inheritance: to every one shall his inheritance be given according to those that were numbered of him.
verses.bsb: Increase the inheritance for a large tribe and decrease it for a small one; each tribe is to receive its inheritance according to the number of those registered.

verses.esv: But the land shall be divided by lot. According to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.
verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the land shall be divided by lot: according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the land must be divided by lot; they shall receive their inheritance according to the names of the tribes of their fathers.

verses.esv: Their inheritance shall be divided according to lot between the larger and the smaller.”
verses.kjv: According to the lot shall the possession thereof be divided between many and few.
verses.bsb: Each inheritance is to be divided by lot among the larger and smaller tribes.”

verses.esv: This was the list of the Levites according to their clans: of Gershon, the clan of the Gershonites; of Kohath, the clan of the Kohathites; of Merari, the clan of the Merarites.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are they that were numbered of the Levites after their families: of Gershon, the family of the Gershonites: of Kohath, the family of the Kohathites: of Merari, the family of the Merarites.
verses.bsb: Now these were the Levites numbered by their clans:  The Gershonite clan from Gershon,   the Kohathite clan from Kohath,   and the Merarite clan from Merari.

verses.esv: These are the clans of Levi: the clan of the Libnites, the clan of the Hebronites, the clan of the Mahlites, the clan of the Mushites, the clan of the Korahites. And Kohath was the father of Amram.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the Levites: the family of the Libnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Mahlites, the family of the Mushites, the family of the Korathites. And Kohath begat Amram.
verses.bsb: These were the families of the Levites:  The Libnite clan,   the Hebronite clan,   the Mahlite clan,   the Mushite clan,   and the Korahite clan.   Now Kohath was the father of Amram,

verses.esv: The name of Amram's wife was Jochebed the daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister.
verses.kjv: And the name of Amram’s wife was Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bare to Levi in Egypt: and she bare unto Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam their sister.
verses.bsb: and Amram’s wife was named Jochebed. She was also a daughter of Levi, born to Levi in Egypt. To Amram she bore Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam.

verses.esv: And to Aaron were born Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.kjv: And unto Aaron was born Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.bsb: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar were born to Aaron,

verses.esv: But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And Nadab and Abihu died, when they offered strange fire before the Lord.
verses.bsb: but Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD.

verses.esv: And those listed were 23,000, every male from a month old and upward. For they were not listed among the people of Israel, because there was no inheritance given to them among the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And those that were numbered of them were twenty and three thousand, all males from a month old and upward: for they were not numbered among the children of Israel, because there was no inheritance given them among the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: The registration of the Levites totaled 23,000, every male a month old or more; they were not numbered among the other Israelites, because no inheritance was given to them among the Israelites.

verses.esv: These were those listed by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who listed the people of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
verses.kjv: ¶ These are they that were numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who numbered the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
verses.bsb: These were the ones numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest when they counted the Israelites on the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho.

verses.esv: But among these there was not one of those listed by Moses and Aaron the priest, who had listed the people of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.
verses.kjv: But among these there was not a man of them whom Moses and Aaron the priest numbered, when they numbered the children of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.
verses.bsb: Among all these, however, there was not one who had been numbered by Moses and Aaron the priest when they counted the Israelites in the Wilderness of Sinai.

verses.esv: For the LORD had said of them, “They shall die in the wilderness.” Not one of them was left, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.kjv: For the Lord had said of them, They shall surely die in the wilderness. And there was not left a man of them, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.bsb: For the LORD had told them that they would surely die in the wilderness. Not one was left except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

verses.esv: Then drew near the daughters of Zelophehad the son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from the clans of Manasseh the son of Joseph. The names of his daughters were: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.kjv: THEN came the daughters of Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh the son of Joseph: and these are the names of his daughters; Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.bsb: Now the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, belonged to the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph. These were the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. They approached

verses.esv: And they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before the chiefs and all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying,
verses.kjv: And they stood before Moses, and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes and all the congregation, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying,
verses.bsb: the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, stood before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the leaders, and the whole congregation, and said,

verses.esv: “Our father died in the wilderness. He was not among the company of those who gathered themselves together against the LORD in the company of Korah, but died for his own sin. And he had no sons.
verses.kjv: Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not in the company of them that gathered themselves together against the Lord in the company of Korah; but died in his own sin, and had no sons.
verses.bsb: “Our father died in the wilderness, but he was not among the followers of Korah who gathered together against the LORD. Instead, he died because of his own sin, and he had no sons.

verses.esv: Why should the name of our father be taken away from his clan because he had no son? Give to us a possession among our father's brothers.”
verses.kjv: Why should the name of our father be done away from among his family, because he hath no son? Give unto us therefore a possession among the brethren of our father.
verses.bsb: Why should the name of our father disappear from his clan because he had no sons? Give us property among our father’s brothers.”

verses.esv: Moses brought their case before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And Moses brought their cause before the Lord.
verses.bsb: So Moses brought their case before the LORD,

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: and the LORD answered him,

verses.esv: “The daughters of Zelophehad are right. You shall give them possession of an inheritance among their father's brothers and transfer the inheritance of their father to them.
verses.kjv: The daughters of Zelophehad speak right: thou shalt surely give them a possession of an inheritance among their father’s brethren; and thou shalt cause the inheritance of their father to pass unto them.
verses.bsb: “The daughters of Zelophehad speak correctly. You certainly must give them property as an inheritance among their father’s brothers, and transfer their father’s inheritance to them.

verses.esv: And you shall speak to the people of Israel, saying, ‘If a man dies and has no son, then you shall transfer his inheritance to his daughter.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a man die, and have no son, then ye shall cause his inheritance to pass unto his daughter.
verses.bsb: Furthermore, you shall say to the Israelites, ‘If a man dies and leaves no son, you are to transfer his inheritance to his daughter.

verses.esv: And if he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.
verses.kjv: And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.
verses.bsb: If he has no daughter, give his inheritance to his brothers.

verses.esv: And if he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to his father's brothers.
verses.kjv: And if he have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his father’s brethren.
verses.bsb: If he has no brothers, give his inheritance to his father’s brothers.

verses.esv: And if his father has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the nearest kinsman of his clan, and he shall possess it. And it shall be for the people of Israel a statute and rule, as the LORD commanded Moses.’”
verses.kjv: And if his father have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his kinsman that is next to him of his family, and he shall possess it: and it shall be unto the children of Israel a statute of judgment, as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: And if his father has no brothers, give his inheritance to the next of kin from his clan, that he may take possession of it. This is to be a statutory ordinance for the Israelites, as the LORD has commanded Moses.’”

verses.esv: The LORD said to Moses, “Go up into this mountain of Abarim and see the land that I have given to the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Get thee up into this mount Abarim, and see the land which I have given unto the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go up this mountain of the Abarim range and see the land that I have given the Israelites.

verses.esv: When you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as your brother Aaron was,
verses.kjv: And when thou hast seen it, thou also shalt be gathered unto thy people, as Aaron thy brother was gathered.
verses.bsb: After you have seen it, you too will be gathered to your people, as your brother Aaron was;

verses.esv: because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin when the congregation quarreled, failing to uphold me as holy at the waters before their eyes.” (These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.)
verses.kjv: For ye rebelled against my commandment in the desert of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the water before their eyes: that is the water of Meribah in Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.
verses.bsb: for when the congregation contended in the Wilderness of Zin, both of you rebelled against My command to show My holiness in their sight regarding the waters.” Those were the waters of Meribah in Kadesh, in the Wilderness of Zin.

verses.esv: Moses spoke to the LORD, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses spake unto the Lord, saying,
verses.bsb: So Moses appealed to the LORD,

verses.esv: “Let the LORD, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation
verses.kjv: Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation,
verses.bsb: “May the LORD, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation

verses.esv: who shall go out before them and come in before them, who shall lead them out and bring them in, that the congregation of the LORD may not be as sheep that have no shepherd.”
